╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Plan to implement                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ OpenRevu Phase 5 — Layers                                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Date: 2026-02-23                                                                                                     │
│ Goal: Add a document-wide layer system: create/rename/delete layers, toggle                                          │
│ visibility and lock state, auto-assign newly drawn objects to the active layer,                                      │
│ persist layer definitions to the server. Also fix the Export button label.                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Context                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Users need to organize markups into named, togglable layers — e.g. "Structural",                                     │
│ "Electrical", "Comments". Currently everything lives on one flat canvas. The                                         │
│ Fabric JSON already survives page navigation via CUSTOM_PROPERTIES serialization;                                    │
│ adding layerId to that list is the only canvas.js change required. The                                               │
│ document_settings table (used by the scale system) is the right store for                                            │
│ layer definitions. The left-panel tab pattern (Pages / Markups / Measures) is                                        │
│ exactly what the Layers panel should follow.                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Data Model                                                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Layer definitions stored as JSON in document_settings key "layers":                                                  │
│ {                                                                                                                    │
│   "layers": [                                                                                                        │
│     { "id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": true, "locked": false }                                         │
│   ],                                                                                                                 │
│   "activeId": "default"                                                                                              │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Each Fabric object gets obj.layerId stamped by LayerManager via object:added.                                        │
│ Serialized automatically via the CUSTOM_PROPERTIES prototype patch in canvas.js.                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Architecture                                                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ LayerManager (layers.js)                                                                                             │
│   _layers[]          — [{id, name, visible, locked}]                                                                 │
│   _activeLayerId     — string                                                                                        │
│   _canvas            — CanvasOverlay ref                                                                             │
│   _fabricCanvas      — current Fabric canvas (reset on each doc load)                                                │
│   _docId                                                                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│   init(canvas)           — store ref, bind #btn-new-layer click                                                      │
│   load(docId)            — GET /api/.../layers, render, _applyAllLayerStates()                                       │
│   initForCanvas(fc)      — rebind object:added handler (called per doc load)                                         │
│   setActive(id)          — highlight row, update _activeLayerId                                                      │
│   toggleVisible(id)      — flip visible, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()                                             │
│   toggleLocked(id)       — flip locked, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()                                              │
│   addLayer()             — push {id:uuid, name:'Layer N'...}; _save(); refresh()                                     │
│   renameLayer(id, name)  — update name, _save()                                                                      │
│   deleteLayer(id)        — guard >=2 needed; orphans->default; _save(); refresh()                                    │
│   _applyLayerState(obj)  — sets obj.visible / selectable / evented from its layer                                    │
│   _applyAllLayerStates() — iterates all fc.getObjects(), applyLayerState, renderAll()                                │
│   _save()                — PUT /api/.../layers {layers, activeId}                                                    │
│   refresh()              — rebuild #layers-list DOM from _layers                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ object:added hook (in initForCanvas(fc)):                                                                            │
│ fc.on('object:added', (e) => {                                                                                       │
│     const obj = e.target;                                                                                            │
│     if (!obj.layerId) obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId; // new objects only                                         │
│     this._applyLayerState(obj);  // always apply visibility/lock                                                     │
│ });                                                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Files Changed                                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ┌──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │

│ ├──────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤     │

│ │ run_tests.mjs            │ Append test_phase5_layers.mjs to TEST_FILES                                       │     │
│ └──────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Detailed Changes                                                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 1. canvas.js (line 64) — Add layerId to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ After 'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints':                                                                                   │
│     'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints',                                                                                     │
│     // Phase 5: layer assignment — which layer this markup belongs to                                                │
│     'layerId',                                                                                                       │
│ No other canvas.js changes. stampDefaults() is NOT touched — layerId assignment                                      │
│ happens in LayerManager's object:added hook, keeping canvas.js fully decoupled.                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ 2. layers.js — NEW                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Layer row HTML (built in refresh(), content via textContent):                                                        │
│ <div class="layer-row [active] [layer-hidden] [layer-locked]" data-layer-id="{id}">                                  │
│   <button class="layer-vis-btn"  title="Toggle visibility">👁</button>                                                │
│   <button class="layer-lock-btn" title="Toggle lock">🔓</button>                                                     │
│   <span class="layer-name" title="Double-click to rename">{name}</span>                                              │
│   <button class="layer-delete-btn" title="Delete layer">×</button>                                                   │
│ </div>                                                                                                               │
│ - active — blue left border (matches markup-row pattern)                                                             │
│ - layer-hidden — dims row + strikethrough name; added when !layer.visible                                            │
│ - layer-locked — amber lock icon; added when layer.locked                                                            │
│ - Delete × appears on hover via CSS opacity                                                                          │
│ - Double-click on .layer-name → replace span with <input>, blur/Enter saves                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Key implementation notes:                                                                                            │
│ - _objectAddedHandler stored on instance so initForCanvas() can fc.off() it on rebind                                │
│ - deleteLayer() re-assigns orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE splicing the array                                   │
│ - load() is async; await it in app.js _onDocumentLoaded so layer state is set                                        │
│ before the 200ms-delayed _loadMarkups() call renders objects onto canvas                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ 3. app.js — Wiring                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Import (after existing imports):                                                                                     │
│ import { LayerManager } from './layers.js';                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Constructor (after this.plugins = new PluginLoader()):                                                               │
│ this.layerManager = new LayerManager();                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _onDocumentLoaded(info) — after this.canvas.init(this.viewer):                                                       │
│ // Layers: init binds the + New Layer button (idempotent guard inside init).                                         │
│ // initForCanvas rebinds object:added on the fresh Fabric canvas instance.                                           │
│ // load fetches layer defs and applies visibility/lock before markups render.                                        │
│ this.layerManager.init(this.canvas);                                                                                 │
│ this.layerManager.initForCanvas(this.canvas.fabricCanvas);                                                           │
│ await this.layerManager.load(info.id);                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ After this.markupList.measureSummary = this.measureSummary:                                                          │
│ this.markupList.layerManager = this.layerManager;                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │

│ 5. style.css — Append                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* =========================================================================                                         │
│  * LAYERS PANEL (Phase 5)                                                                                            │
│  * ========================================================================= */                                      │
│ #layers-header {                                                                                                     │
│     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;                                                                    │
│     padding: 6px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid #3a3a50; flex-shrink: 0;                                              │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ #layers-count { font-size: 11px; color: #8888a0; font-weight: 600; flex: 1; }                                        │
│ .layers-new-btn { font-size: 11px !important; padding: 2px 8px !important; }                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ .layers-list { overflow-y: auto; flex: 1; }                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ .layer-row {                                                                                                         │
│     display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px;                                                                    │
│     padding: 5px 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a3d;                                                              │
│     cursor: pointer; font-size: 12px; color: #c0c0cc;                                                                │
│     transition: background 0.12s;                                                                                    │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ .layer-row:hover   { background: #2a2a3d; }                                                                          │
│ .layer-row.active  { background: #2a3a55; border-left: 3px solid #4a9eff; padding-left: 5px; }                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ .layer-vis-btn, .layer-lock-btn, .layer-delete-btn {                                                                 │
│     background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer;                                                                 │
│     color: #8888a0; font-size: 12px; padding: 1px 3px; flex-shrink: 0;                                               │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ .layer-vis-btn:hover, .layer-lock-btn:hover { color: #c0c0cc; }                                                      │
│ .layer-delete-btn { margin-left: auto; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.1s; }                                       │
│ .layer-row:hover .layer-delete-btn { opacity: 1; }                                                                   │
│ .layer-delete-btn:hover { color: #ff4444; }                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ .layer-name {                                                                                                        │
│     flex: 1; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis;                                         │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ .layer-name-input {                                                                                                  │
│     flex: 1; background: #1e1e2e; border: 1px solid #4a9eff;                                                         │
│     color: #e0e0e8; font-size: 12px; padding: 1px 4px; border-radius: 2px;                                           │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* Hidden layer: dim row + strikethrough name */                                                                     │
│ .layer-row.layer-hidden  { opacity: 0.45; }                                                                          │
│ .layer-row.layer-hidden .layer-name { text-decoration: line-through; }                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /* Locked layer: amber lock icon */                                                                                  │
│ .layer-row.layer-locked .layer-lock-btn { color: #ffaa00; }                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │

│ 7. main.py — Layer API                                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Add before the ENTRY POINT section:                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ # =============================================================================                                      │
│ # API ROUTES — LAYERS                                                                                                │
│ # =============================================================================                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ def _default_layers() -> dict:                                                                                       │
│     """Return the default single-layer configuration for new documents."""                                           │
│     return {                                                                                                         │
│         "layers": [{"id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": True, "locked": False}],                          │
│         "activeId": "default",                                                                                       │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ @app.get("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")                                                                           │
│ async def get_layers(doc_id: int):                                                                                   │
│     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)                                                                                    │
│     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")                                                       │
│     raw = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "layers")                                                                  │
│     if raw:                                                                                                          │
│         import json as _j                                                                                            │
│         try: return JSONResponse(_j.loads(raw))                                                                      │
│         except Exception: pass                                                                                       │
│     return JSONResponse(_default_layers())                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ @app.put("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")                                                                           │
│ async def put_layers(doc_id: int, request: Request):                                                                 │
│     doc = db.get_document(doc_id)                                                                                    │
│     if not doc: raise HTTPException(404, "Document not found")                                                       │
│     body = await request.json()                                                                                      │
│     layers = body.get("layers")                                                                                      │
│     if not isinstance(layers, list) or len(layers) == 0:                                                             │
│         raise HTTPException(400, "layers must be a non-empty list")                                                  │
│     # SECURITY: clamp strings, validate types                                                                        │
│     cleaned = []                                                                                                     │
│     for layer in layers:                                                                                             │
│         if not isinstance(layer, dict): continue                                                                     │
│         cleaned.append({                                                                                             │
│             "id":      str(layer.get("id", "default"))[:64],                                                         │
│             "name":    str(layer.get("name", "Layer"))[:64],                                                         │
│             "visible": bool(layer.get("visible", True)),                                                             │
│             "locked":  bool(layer.get("locked", False)),                                                             │
│         })                                                                                                           │
│     if not cleaned:                                                                                                  │
│         raise HTTPException(400, "No valid layers provided")                                                         │
│     active_id = str(body.get("activeId", "default"))[:64]                                                            │
│     import json as _j                                                                                                │
│     db.set_document_setting(doc_id, "layers", _j.dumps({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id}))                  │
│     return JSONResponse({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id})                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Test Plan — test_phase5_layers.mjs (25 tests)                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 1: Panel Shell (5)                                                                                             │
│ - Layers tab button exists in .panel-tabs                                                                            │
│ - #tab-layers content div exists                                                                                     │
│ - Clicking Layers tab makes it .active                                                                               │
│ - #layers-list exists in panel                                                                                       │
│ - #btn-new-layer button exists                                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 2: Server Endpoint (5)                                                                                         │
│ - GET /api/documents/1/layers → 200                                                                                  │
│ - Response has non-empty layers array                                                                                │
│ - Response has activeId string field                                                                                 │
│ - Default layer has id = "default" and name = "Default"                                                              │
│ - Default layer has visible: true, locked: false                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 3: Layer CRUD (7)                                                                                              │
│ - Clicking + Layer adds a new .layer-row to #layers-list                                                             │
│ - #layers-count text increments after adding a layer                                                                 │
│ - Double-click layer name → <input> appears; type + blur → name updates in DOM                                       │
│ - Renamed layer name is saved to server (GET /api/.../layers returns new name)                                       │
│ - Delete × button removes the layer row from the list                                                                │
│ - Cannot delete last remaining layer (guard: ≥2 required)                                                            │
│ - After add + delete, remaining layer count is correct                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 4: Layer States (5)                                                                                            │
│ - Clicking a layer row gives it .active class (removes .active from others)                                          │
│ - Eye 👁 button toggles → row gains .layer-hidden class                                                               │
│ - Objects on a hidden layer: obj.visible === false on the Fabric canvas                                              │
│ - Lock 🔓 button toggles → row gains .layer-locked class                                                             │
│ - Objects on a locked layer: obj.selectable === false on the Fabric canvas                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Group 5: Markup Assignment (3)                                                                                       │
│ - New markup drawn when Default is active → obj.layerId === 'default'                                                │
│ - New markup drawn when a non-default layer is active → obj.layerId === <that id>                                    │
│ - After page navigation and return, layerId is preserved on canvas objects                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Target: 438 + 25 = 463 passing                                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Implementation Order                                                                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ 1. static/js/canvas.js — add 'layerId' to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES                                                          │
│ 2. main.py — _default_layers() + GET + PUT endpoints                                                                 │
│ 3. static/js/layers.js — NEW — LayerManager class                                                                    │
│ 4. static/js/markup-list.js — add layers case to _bindTabSwitching()                                                 │
│ 5. templates/editor.html — tab button + content div + Export label fix                                               │
│ 6. static/css/style.css — append layer styles                                                                        │
│ 7. static/js/app.js — import + constructor + _onDocumentLoaded wiring                                                │
│ 8. test_phase5_layers.mjs — NEW                                                                                      │
│ 9. run_tests.mjs — add suite                                                                                         │
│ 10. Restart server + run full suite (target: 463+)                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Gotchas                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ - initForCanvas(fc) must store handler as this._objectAddedHandler and call                                          │
│ fc.off('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler) before rebinding — Fabric uses                                      │
│ named function references for off().                                                                                 │
│ - object:added fires during loadFromJSON for objects that already have layerId;                                      │
│ skip assignment (if (!obj.layerId)) but always call _applyLayerState(obj).                                           │
│ - load(docId) is async — await it in _onDocumentLoaded so layer defs are ready                                       │
│ before the 200ms setTimeout fires for _loadMarkups().                                                                │
│ - deleteLayer() must reassign orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE splicing the                                      │
│ array, otherwise _applyAllLayerStates() can't find the old layer.                                                    │
│ - _bindTabSwitching() uses querySelectorAll('.panel-tab') at bind time — the Layers                                  │
│ tab <button> must be in the static HTML so it's captured when init() runs.                                           │
│ - Lock state does NOT hide objects — visible stays true, only selectable + evented                                   │
│ go false. Hidden + locked is the intersection of both rules.                                                         │
│ - Export label fix: change &#128190; Export → &#128190; Export PDF in editor.html.                                   │

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The layer system uses the same document_settings table as the scale system — a generic key-value store that avoids
  schema migrations. The object:added hook strategy keeps LayerManager fully decoupled from canvas.js, since canvas.js
  never needs to know layers exist. layerId gets its own spot in CUSTOM_PROPERTIES so it serializes through the existing
   Fabric JSON pipeline for free.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Let me now implement all the changes in order:

  Step 1: canvas.js — add layerId to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES

       977  # =============================================================================
       978 +# API ROUTES — LAYERS (Phase 5)
       979 +# =============================================================================
       980 +
       981 +def _default_layers() -> dict:
       982 +    """
       983 +    Return the default single-layer configuration for new documents.
       984 +
       985 +    Every document starts with a single 'Default' layer that is visible
       986 +    and unlocked. This is the baseline state before any user customization.
       987 +
       988 +    Returns:
       989 +        Dict with 'layers' list and 'activeId' string.
       990 +    """
       991 +    return {
       992 +        "layers": [{"id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": True, "locked": False}],
       993 +        "activeId": "default",
       994 +    }
       995 +
       996 +
       997 +@app.get("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
       998 +async def get_layers(doc_id: int):
       999 +    """
      1000 +    Get the layer definitions for a document.
      1001 +
      1002 +    Returns the full layer configuration: a list of layer objects
      1003 +    with id/name/visible/locked, plus the currently active layer ID.
      1004 +    If no layer configuration has been saved, returns the default
      1005 +    single-layer setup.
      1006 +
      1007 +    Args:
      1008 +        doc_id: Document database ID.
      1009 +
      1010 +    Returns:
      1011 +        { "layers": [...], "activeId": str }
      1012 +    """
      1013 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      1014 +    if not doc:
      1015 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Document not found")
      1016 +
      1017 +    raw = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "layers")
      1018 +    if raw:
      1019 +        import json as _j
      1020 +        try:
      1021 +            return JSONResponse(_j.loads(raw))
      1022 +        except Exception:
      1023 +            pass
      1024 +
      1025 +    # No saved layers — return default configuration
      1026 +    return JSONResponse(_default_layers())
      1027 +
      1028 +
      1029 +@app.put("/api/documents/{doc_id}/layers")
      1030 +async def put_layers(doc_id: int, request: Request):
      1031 +    """
      1032 +    Save the layer definitions for a document.
      1033 +
      1034 +    Expects JSON body: { "layers": [...], "activeId": str }
      1035 +
      1036 +    Each layer must be a dict with id, name, visible, locked.
      1037 +    At least one layer is required. Strings are clamped to 64 chars
      1038 +    to prevent excessively large payloads.
      1039 +
      1040 +    Security:
      1041 +        - Validates document exists before writing
      1042 +        - Clamps string lengths to prevent oversized storage
      1043 +        - Type-validates all layer fields before persisting
      1044 +        - Rejects empty layers list (document must always have ≥1 layer)
      1045 +
      1046 +    Args:
      1047 +        doc_id: Document database ID.
      1048 +
      1049 +    Returns:
      1050 +        The cleaned layer configuration that was saved.
      1051 +    """
      1052 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      1053 +    if not doc:
      1054 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Document not found")
      1055 +
      1056 +    body = await request.json()
      1057 +    layers = body.get("layers")
      1058 +
      1059 +    if not isinstance(layers, list) or len(layers) == 0:
      1060 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="layers must be a non-empty list")
      1061 +
      1062 +    # SECURITY: clamp strings, validate types — reject malformed layer objects
      1063 +    cleaned = []
      1064 +    for layer in layers:
      1065 +        if not isinstance(layer, dict):
      1066 +            continue
      1067 +        cleaned.append({
      1068 +            "id":      str(layer.get("id", "default"))[:64],
      1069 +            "name":    str(layer.get("name", "Layer"))[:64],
      1070 +            "visible": bool(layer.get("visible", True)),
      1071 +            "locked":  bool(layer.get("locked", False)),
      1072 +        })
      1073 +
      1074 +    if not cleaned:
      1075 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No valid layers provided")
      1076 +
      1077 +    active_id = str(body.get("activeId", "default"))[:64]
      1078 +
      1079 +    import json as _j
      1080 +    db.set_document_setting(
      1081 +        doc_id, "layers",
      1082 +        _j.dumps({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id})
      1083 +    )
      1084 +
      1085 +    return JSONResponse({"layers": cleaned, "activeId": active_id})
      1086 +
      1087 +
      1088 +# =============================================================================
      1089  # ENTRY POINT
      1090  # =============================================================================
      1091

● Step 3: Create layers.js — LayerManager class

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The _objectAddedHandler is stored as an instance property so initForCanvas() can cleanly fc.off() it before rebinding
  — Fabric.js requires named function references for event removal. Without this, each document load would accumulate
  duplicate handlers, causing objects to be assigned to wrong layers. The async load() pattern ensures layer state is
  applied before loadFromJSON fires during _loadMarkups().
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/layers.js)
  ⎿  Wrote 564 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/layers.js
        1 /**
        2  * OpenRevu — Layer Manager Module (Phase 5)
        3  *
        4  * Purpose:
        5  *   Document-wide layer system for organizing markups into named, togglable
        6  *   layers (e.g. "Structural", "Electrical", "Comments"). Provides create,
        7  *   rename, delete, visibility toggle, and lock operations. Layer definitions
        8  *   persist to the server via document_settings. Each Fabric object is
        9  *   auto-assigned to the active layer when drawn.
       10  *
       11  * Architecture:
       12  *   - Layer defs stored in memory as _layers[] and persisted as JSON in the
       13  *     document_settings table (key "layers") — same store as scale settings.
       14  *   - Fabric objects get layerId stamped via object:added hook in initForCanvas().
       15  *   - Visibility/lock state applied by iterating all canvas objects whenever
       16  *     a layer state changes. This is O(n) in objects but document markups are
       17  *     small enough that it's imperceptible.
       18  *   - initForCanvas() is idempotent: removes old handler before rebinding
       19  *     so document reload doesn't accumulate duplicate handlers.
       20  *
       21  * Security:
       22  *   - Layer names rendered via textContent (never innerHTML) — XSS safe
       23  *   - Layer IDs generated with crypto.randomUUID() — not user-controlled
       24  *   - Server validates and clamps all layer fields on PUT
       25  *
       26  * Author: OpenRevu Team
       27  * Version: 1.0.0
       28  * Date: 2026-02-23
       29  */
       30
       31 // =============================================================================
       32 // LAYER MANAGER
       33 // =============================================================================
       34
       35 /**
       36  * Manages the layer panel and layer state for a document.
       37  *
       38  * Usage:
       39  *   const lm = new LayerManager();
       40  *   lm.init(canvas);                           // bind UI, idempotent
       41  *   lm.initForCanvas(canvas.fabricCanvas);     // rebind object:added per doc
       42  *   await lm.load(docId);                      // fetch from server, apply state
       43  *
       44  * Layer object shape:
       45  *   { id: string, name: string, visible: boolean, locked: boolean }
       46  */
       47 export class LayerManager {
       48     constructor() {
       49         /** @type {Array<{id:string, name:string, visible:boolean, locked:boolean}>} */
       50         this._layers = [];
       51
       52         /** @type {string} ID of the currently active layer */
       53         this._activeLayerId = 'default';
       54
       55         /** @type {import('./canvas.js').CanvasOverlay|null} */
       56         this._canvas = null;
       57
       58         /** @type {fabric.Canvas|null} Active Fabric canvas instance */
       59         this._fabricCanvas = null;
       60
       61         /** @type {number|null} Current document ID */
       62         this._docId = null;
       63
       64         /**
       65          * Stored reference to the object:added handler so initForCanvas()
       66          * can remove it before rebinding on document reload.
       67          * Fabric.js off() requires the exact same function reference used in on().
       68          * @type {Function|null}
       69          */
       70         this._objectAddedHandler = null;
       71
       72         /** Guard: prevent double-binding the #btn-new-layer click */
       73         this._initBound = false;
       74     }
       75
       76     // =========================================================================
       77     // INITIALIZATION
       78     // =========================================================================
       79
       80     /**
       81      * Bind UI elements that don't change between document loads.
       82      * Safe to call multiple times — idempotent guard via _initBound.
       83      *
       84      * Args:
       85      *   canvas: CanvasOverlay instance (used for fabricCanvas access).
       86      */
       87     init(canvas) {
       88         this._canvas = canvas;
       89
       90         if (this._initBound) return;
       91         this._initBound = true;
       92
       93         const btnNew = document.getElementById('btn-new-layer');
       94         if (btnNew) {
       95             btnNew.addEventListener('click', () => this.addLayer());
       96         }
       97
       98         console.log('[LayerManager] UI bound');
       99     }
      100
      101     /**
      102      * Rebind the object:added handler to a fresh Fabric canvas instance.
      103      *
      104      * Called each time a new document is loaded because canvas.init()
      105      * creates a new fabric.Canvas object. Removes the previous handler
      106      * first to prevent accumulation across document loads.
      107      *
      108      * The handler does two things:
      109      *   1. Assigns layerId to newly drawn objects (if not already set)
      110      *   2. Applies visibility/lock state to all objects (including loaded ones)
      111      *
      112      * Args:
      113      *   fc: fabric.Canvas — the newly created Fabric canvas instance.
      114      */
      115     initForCanvas(fc) {
      116         // Remove old handler before rebinding (Fabric off() needs exact ref)
      117         if (this._fabricCanvas && this._objectAddedHandler) {
      118             this._fabricCanvas.off('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler);
      119         }
      120
      121         this._fabricCanvas = fc;
      122
      123         // Build and store handler so we can remove it on next rebind
      124         this._objectAddedHandler = (e) => {
      125             const obj = e.target;
      126             if (!obj) return;
      127
      128             // Assign layer only to newly drawn objects — loaded objects already have layerId
      129             if (!obj.layerId) {
      130                 obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId;
      131             }
      132
      133             // Always apply current visibility/lock state (handles loaded objects too)
      134             this._applyLayerState(obj);
      135         };
      136
      137         fc.on('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler);
      138         console.log('[LayerManager] object:added bound to new Fabric canvas');
      139     }
      140
      141     // =========================================================================
      142     // LOAD / SAVE
      143     // =========================================================================
      144
      145     /**
      146      * Load layer definitions from the server for a document.
      147      *
      148      * Fetches the layer config, populates _layers and _activeLayerId,
      149      * then renders the panel and applies state to any objects already on
      150      * the canvas. Called with await in app.js _onDocumentLoaded() so layers
      151      * are ready before _loadMarkups() fires.
      152      *
      153      * Args:
      154      *   docId: Document database ID.
      155      */
      156     async load(docId) {
      157         this._docId = docId;
      158         try {
      159             const resp = await fetch(`/api/documents/${docId}/layers`);
      160             if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${resp.status}`);
      161             const data = await resp.json();
      162
      163             this._layers = Array.isArray(data.layers) ? data.layers : [];
      164             this._activeLayerId = data.activeId || 'default';
      165
      166             // Ensure there's always at least a Default layer (defensive)
      167             if (this._layers.length === 0) {
      168                 this._layers = [{ id: 'default', name: 'Default', visible: true, locked: false }];
      169                 this._activeLayerId = 'default';
      170             }
      171
      172             this.refresh();
      173
      174             // Apply layer state to any objects already on the canvas
      175             // (e.g., if layers were loaded after markups in a timing edge case)
      176             this._applyAllLayerStates();
      177
      178             console.log(`[LayerManager] Loaded ${this._layers.length} layers for doc ${docId}`);
      179         } catch (err) {
      180             console.error('[LayerManager] Failed to load layers:', err);
      181             // Fallback to default single layer
      182             this._layers = [{ id: 'default', name: 'Default', visible: true, locked: false }];
      183             this._activeLayerId = 'default';
      184             this.refresh();
      185         }
      186     }
      187
      188     /**
      189      * Persist current layer definitions to the server.
      190      *
      191      * Uses PUT /api/documents/{id}/layers with the full layer array and
      192      * current activeId. Called after any state-changing operation
      193      * (add, delete, rename, toggle visible/locked).
      194      *
      195      * Failures are logged but do not throw — layer operations feel instant
      196      * to the user even if the network lags. The next save will retry the
      197      * full current state.
      198      */
      199     async _save() {
      200         if (!this._docId) return;
      201         try {
      202             await fetch(`/api/documents/${this._docId}/layers`, {
      203                 method: 'PUT',
      204                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      205                 body: JSON.stringify({
      206                     layers: this._layers,
      207                     activeId: this._activeLayerId,
      208                 }),
      209             });
      210         } catch (err) {
      211             console.error('[LayerManager] Failed to save layers:', err);
      212         }
      213     }
      214
      215     // =========================================================================
      216     // LAYER CRUD
      217     // =========================================================================
      218
      219     /**
      220      * Set a layer as the active drawing target.
      221      *
      222      * Updates the visual highlight in the panel. All subsequent objects
      223      * drawn on the canvas will be assigned to this layer.
      224      *
      225      * Args:
      226      *   id: Layer ID string.
      227      */
      228     setActive(id) {
      229         this._activeLayerId = id;
      230         // Update DOM — highlight active row
      231         const rows = document.querySelectorAll('#layers-list .layer-row');
      232         rows.forEach(row => {
      233             row.classList.toggle('active', row.dataset.layerId === id);
      234         });
      235     }
      236
      237     /**
      238      * Toggle a layer's visibility.
      239      *
      240      * When hidden: objects on this layer become invisible (obj.visible = false).
      241      * When shown: objects become visible again.
      242      * State is saved to the server after every toggle.
      243      *
      244      * Args:
      245      *   id: Layer ID string.
      246      */
      247     toggleVisible(id) {
      248         const layer = this._layers.find(l => l.id === id);
      249         if (!layer) return;
      250         layer.visible = !layer.visible;
      251         this._applyAllLayerStates();
      252         this._save();
      253         this.refresh();
      254     }
      255
      256     /**
      257      * Toggle a layer's lock state.
      258      *
      259      * When locked: objects are not selectable or evented (can't be moved).
      260      * When unlocked: objects become interactive again.
      261      * Visibility is not affected by lock state.
      262      *
      263      * Args:
      264      *   id: Layer ID string.
      265      */
      266     toggleLocked(id) {
      267         const layer = this._layers.find(l => l.id === id);
      268         if (!layer) return;
      269         layer.locked = !layer.locked;
      270         this._applyAllLayerStates();
      271         this._save();
      272         this.refresh();
      273     }
      274
      275     /**
      276      * Add a new layer to the document.
      277      *
      278      * Creates a layer with a UUID, auto-generated name "Layer N",
      279      * visible=true, locked=false. Saves and refreshes the panel.
      280      */
      281     addLayer() {
      282         const n = this._layers.length + 1;
      283         const newLayer = {
      284             id: crypto.randomUUID(),
      285             name: `Layer ${n}`,
      286             visible: true,
      287             locked: false,
      288         };
      289         this._layers.push(newLayer);
      290         this._save();
      291         this.refresh();
      292         console.log(`[LayerManager] Added layer: ${newLayer.name} (${newLayer.id})`);
      293     }
      294
      295     /**
      296      * Rename a layer.
      297      *
      298      * Updates the layer name in memory and saves. The panel is not
      299      * fully re-rendered — the caller manages DOM inline-edit cleanup.
      300      *
      301      * Args:
      302      *   id:   Layer ID string.
      303      *   name: New name string (will be trimmed).
      304      */
      305     renameLayer(id, name) {
      306         const layer = this._layers.find(l => l.id === id);
      307         if (!layer) return;
      308         const trimmed = name.trim();
      309         if (!trimmed) return; // Reject empty names
      310         layer.name = trimmed;
      311         this._save();
      312         console.log(`[LayerManager] Renamed layer ${id} → "${trimmed}"`);
      313     }
      314
      315     /**
      316      * Delete a layer.
      317      *
      318      * Guard: cannot delete the last remaining layer. Orphaned objects
      319      * (those whose layerId was the deleted layer) are re-assigned to
      320      * 'default' BEFORE splicing the array so _applyAllLayerStates()
      321      * can find the default layer.
      322      *
      323      * Args:
      324      *   id: Layer ID string.
      325      */
      326     deleteLayer(id) {
      327         if (this._layers.length <= 1) {
      328             console.warn('[LayerManager] Cannot delete last layer');
      329             return;
      330         }
      331
      332         // Re-assign orphaned objects to 'default' BEFORE removing the layer
      333         // so _applyAllLayerStates() can resolve the default layer state
      334         if (this._fabricCanvas) {
      335             for (const obj of this._fabricCanvas.getObjects()) {
      336                 if (obj.layerId === id) {
      337                     obj.layerId = 'default';
      338                 }
      339             }
      340         }
      341         // Also re-assign orphans in serialized pages
      342         if (this._canvas) {
      343             for (const [, fabricJson] of this._canvas.pageMarkups) {
      344                 if (!fabricJson || !fabricJson.objects) continue;
      345                 for (const obj of fabricJson.objects) {
      346                     if (obj.layerId === id) obj.layerId = 'default';
      347                 }
      348             }
      349         }
      350
      351         this._layers = this._layers.filter(l => l.id !== id);
      352
      353         // If the active layer was deleted, fall back to first remaining layer
      354         if (this._activeLayerId === id) {
      355             this._activeLayerId = this._layers[0].id;
      356         }
      357
      358         this._applyAllLayerStates();
      359         this._save();
      360         this.refresh();
      361         console.log(`[LayerManager] Deleted layer ${id}`);
      362     }
      363
      364     // =========================================================================
      365     // CANVAS STATE APPLICATION
      366     // =========================================================================
      367
      368     /**
      369      * Apply a single layer's visibility and lock state to one Fabric object.
      370      *
      371      * Visibility and lock are orthogonal:
      372      *   - Hidden: obj.visible = false (invisible, unselectable, unevented)
      373      *   - Locked: obj.selectable = false, obj.evented = false (but still visible)
      374      *   - Hidden + Locked: all three flags false (intersection of both rules)
      375      *
      376      * Args:
      377      *   obj: Fabric.js object with a layerId property.
      378      */
      379     _applyLayerState(obj) {
      380         if (!obj.layerId) return;
      381         const layer = this._layers.find(l => l.id === obj.layerId);
      382         if (!layer) return; // Orphaned object — leave as-is
      383
      384         const hidden = !layer.visible;
      385         const locked = layer.locked;
      386
      387         obj.visible = !hidden;
      388         obj.selectable = !hidden && !locked;
      389         obj.evented = !hidden && !locked;
      390     }
      391
      392     /**
      393      * Apply all layer states to every object on the current Fabric canvas.
      394      *
      395      * Called after any layer state change (toggle visible/locked, delete).
      396      * O(n) in canvas objects — fast enough for typical document markups.
      397      */
      398     _applyAllLayerStates() {
      399         if (!this._fabricCanvas) return;
      400         for (const obj of this._fabricCanvas.getObjects()) {
      401             this._applyLayerState(obj);
      402         }
      403         this._fabricCanvas.renderAll();
      404     }
      405
      406     // =========================================================================
      407     // PANEL RENDERING
      408     // =========================================================================
      409
      410     /**
      411      * Rebuild the #layers-list DOM from _layers[].
      412      *
      413      * Called after any state change that affects the list.
      414      * Uses textContent for all user-supplied strings to prevent XSS.
      415      */
      416     refresh() {
      417         const list = document.getElementById('layers-list');
      418         const countEl = document.getElementById('layers-count');
      419         const emptyEl = document.getElementById('layers-empty');
      420         if (!list) return;
      421
      422         // Update count label
      423         if (countEl) {
      424             countEl.textContent = `${this._layers.length} layer${this._layers.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`
          ;
      425         }
      426
      427         // Show empty message if no layers (shouldn't happen but defensive)
      428         if (emptyEl) {
      429             emptyEl.style.display = this._layers.length === 0 ? '' : 'none';
      430         }
      431
      432         // Rebuild rows — clear only the rows, not the empty-msg placeholder
      433         const oldRows = list.querySelectorAll('.layer-row');
      434         oldRows.forEach(r => r.remove());
      435
      436         for (const layer of this._layers) {
      437             list.appendChild(this._buildRow(layer));
      438         }
      439     }
      440
      441     /**
      442      * Build a single layer row DOM element.
      443      *
      444      * Row layout:
      445      *   [eye btn] [lock btn] [name span] ... [delete btn]
      446      *
      447      * All text set via textContent (XSS safe).
      448      * Delete button only appears on hover via CSS opacity.
      449      * Double-click on name → inline rename input.
      450      *
      451      * Args:
      452      *   layer: Layer object { id, name, visible, locked }
      453      *
      454      * Returns:
      455      *   HTMLElement — the built row div.
      456      */
      457     _buildRow(layer) {
      458         const row = document.createElement('div');
      459         row.className = 'layer-row';
      460         row.dataset.layerId = layer.id;
      461         if (layer.id === this._activeLayerId) row.classList.add('active');
      462         if (!layer.visible) row.classList.add('layer-hidden');
      463         if (layer.locked) row.classList.add('layer-locked');
      464
      465         // Visibility toggle button
      466         const visBtn = document.createElement('button');
      467         visBtn.className = 'layer-vis-btn';
      468         visBtn.title = 'Toggle visibility';
      469         visBtn.textContent = '👁';
      470         visBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
      471             e.stopPropagation(); // Don't trigger row setActive
      472             this.toggleVisible(layer.id);
      473         });
      474
      475         // Lock toggle button
      476         const lockBtn = document.createElement('button');
      477         lockBtn.className = 'layer-lock-btn';
      478         lockBtn.title = 'Toggle lock';
      479         lockBtn.textContent = layer.locked ? '🔒' : '🔓';
      480         lockBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
      481             e.stopPropagation();
      482             this.toggleLocked(layer.id);
      483         });
      484
      485         // Layer name span — double-click to rename
      486         const nameSpan = document.createElement('span');
      487         nameSpan.className = 'layer-name';
      488         nameSpan.title = 'Double-click to rename';
      489         nameSpan.textContent = layer.name; // textContent: XSS safe
      490         nameSpan.addEventListener('dblclick', (e) => {
      491             e.stopPropagation();
      492             this._startRename(layer.id, nameSpan);
      493         });
      494
      495         // Delete button — only shown on hover via CSS
      496         const deleteBtn = document.createElement('button');
      497         deleteBtn.className = 'layer-delete-btn';
      498         deleteBtn.title = 'Delete layer';
      499         deleteBtn.textContent = '×';
      500         deleteBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
      501             e.stopPropagation();
      502             this.deleteLayer(layer.id);
      503         });
      504
      505         // Click row → set active (make this the drawing target layer)
      506         row.addEventListener('click', () => this.setActive(layer.id));
      507
      508         row.appendChild(visBtn);
      509         row.appendChild(lockBtn);
      510         row.appendChild(nameSpan);
      511         row.appendChild(deleteBtn);
      512
      513         return row;
      514     }
      515
      516     /**
      517      * Start an inline rename edit on a layer name span.
      518      *
      519      * Replaces the <span> with an <input>. Commits on blur or Enter.
      520      * Cancels (restores original name) on Escape.
      521      *
      522      * Args:
      523      *   id:       Layer ID.
      524      *   nameSpan: The <span class="layer-name"> element to replace.
      525      */
      526     _startRename(id, nameSpan) {
      527         const layer = this._layers.find(l => l.id === id);
      528         if (!layer) return;
      529
      530         const input = document.createElement('input');
      531         input.className = 'layer-name-input';
      532         input.type = 'text';
      533         input.value = layer.name;
      534
      535         const commit = () => {
      536             const newName = input.value.trim();
      537             if (newName) {
      538                 this.renameLayer(id, newName);
      539                 nameSpan.textContent = newName; // Update span without full refresh
      540             }
      541             // Restore span (remove input)
      542             if (input.parentNode) {
      543                 input.parentNode.replaceChild(nameSpan, input);
      544             }
      545         };
      546
      547         const cancel = () => {
      548             if (input.parentNode) {
      549                 input.parentNode.replaceChild(nameSpan, input);
      550             }
      551         };
      552
      553         input.addEventListener('blur', commit);
      554         input.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
      555             if (e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); commit(); }
      556             if (e.key === 'Escape') { e.preventDefault(); cancel(); }
      557         });
      558
      559         // Replace span with input, then focus
      560         nameSpan.parentNode.replaceChild(input, nameSpan);
      561         input.focus();
      562         input.select();
      563     }
      564 }
