        4 +**Goal:** Add a portable save format (`.orevu` ZIP bundle) that preserves the
        5 +original PDF plus all markups, layers, and scale settings for re-editing on
        6 +other machines. Then write a MechDraw AI plugin spec to the PKM vault.
        7
        8  ---
        9
       10  ## Context
       11
       12 -Users need to organize markups into named, togglable layers — e.g. "Structural",
       13 -"Electrical", "Comments". Currently everything lives on one flat canvas. The
       14 -Fabric JSON already survives page navigation via CUSTOM_PROPERTIES serialization;
       15 -adding `layerId` to that list is the only canvas.js change required. The
       16 -`document_settings` table (used by the scale system) is the right store for
       17 -layer definitions. The left-panel tab pattern (Pages / Markups / Measures) is
       18 -exactly what the Layers panel should follow.
       12 +Phase 5 is complete (464/464 tests). The only persistence format currently is
       13 +PDF export (markups baked in, no longer editable) or keeping the SQLite database
       14 +alongside the `data/projects/` folder. Users want to share work-in-progress
       15 +reviews with colleagues who can pick up exactly where the reviewer left off.
       16
       17 +A `.orevu` ZIP bundle is the minimal solution: it is self-contained, human-
       18 +inspectable (rename to `.zip` to browse), and the import path is a thin wrapper
       19 +around existing `db.add_document`, `db.save_markups`, and
       20 +`db.set_document_setting` calls.
       21 +
       22  ---
       23
       22 -## Data Model
       24 +## Bundle Format
       25
       24 -Layer definitions stored as JSON in `document_settings` key `"layers"`:
       25 -```json
       26 -{
       27 -  "layers": [
       28 -    { "id": "default", "name": "Default", "visible": true, "locked": false }
       29 -  ],
       30 -  "activeId": "default"
       31 -}
       32 -```
       26 +`.orevu` is a ZIP (DEFLATED) containing exactly 5 files:
       27
       34 -Each Fabric object gets `obj.layerId` stamped by LayerManager via `object:added`.
       35 -Serialized automatically via the CUSTOM_PROPERTIES prototype patch in canvas.js.
       28 +| File | Contents |
       29 +|------|----------|
       30 +| `metadata.json` | `{version, app, filename, page_count, exported_at}` |
       31 +| `original.pdf` | Byte-exact copy of the source PDF |
       32 +| `markups.json` | `{pages: {"0": fabricJSON, ...}}` — same as markups API |
       33 +| `layers.json` | `{layers:[...], activeId}` — same as layers API |
       34 +| `scale.json` | `{preset, paper_inches_per_unit, label}` — same as scale API |
       35
       36 +Only `metadata.json` and `original.pdf` are required; the others are optional
       37 +(import falls back to defaults if absent). This keeps bundles valid even if the
       38 +user exports from a version that predates layers or scale.
       39 +
       40  ---
       41
       42  ## Architecture
       43
       41 -```
       42 -LayerManager (layers.js)
       43 -  _layers[]          — [{id, name, visible, locked}]
       44 -  _activeLayerId     — string
       45 -  _canvas            — CanvasOverlay ref
       46 -  _fabricCanvas      — current Fabric canvas (reset on each doc load)
       47 -  _docId
       44 +### Backend (main.py)
       45
       49 -  init(canvas)           — store ref, bind #btn-new-layer click
       50 -  load(docId)            — GET /api/.../layers, render, _applyAllLayerStates()
       51 -  initForCanvas(fc)      — rebind object:added handler (called per doc load)
       52 -  setActive(id)          — highlight row, update _activeLayerId
       53 -  toggleVisible(id)      — flip visible, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
       54 -  toggleLocked(id)       — flip locked, _applyAllLayerStates(), _save()
       55 -  addLayer()             — push {id:uuid, name:'Layer N'...}; _save(); refresh()
       56 -  renameLayer(id, name)  — update name, _save()
       57 -  deleteLayer(id)        — guard >=2 needed; orphans->default; _save(); refresh()
       58 -  _applyLayerState(obj)  — sets obj.visible / selectable / evented from its layer
       59 -  _applyAllLayerStates() — iterates all fc.getObjects(), applyLayerState, renderAll()
       60 -  _save()                — PUT /api/.../layers {layers, activeId}
       61 -  refresh()              — rebuild #layers-list DOM from _layers
       46 +**GET `/api/documents/{doc_id}/export-bundle`**
       47  ```
       63 -
       64 -**object:added hook** (in `initForCanvas(fc)`):
       65 -```javascript
       66 -fc.on('object:added', (e) => {
       67 -    const obj = e.target;
       68 -    if (!obj.layerId) obj.layerId = this._activeLayerId; // new objects only
       69 -    this._applyLayerState(obj);  // always apply visibility/lock
       70 -});
       48 +1. Verify doc + PDF file exist
       49 +2. Load: markups (db.get_markups), layers (db.get_document_setting 'layers'),
       50 +         scale (db.get_document_setting 'scale')
       51 +3. Build in-memory zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(), 'w', ZIP_DEFLATED):
       52 +     - writestr("metadata.json", ...)
       53 +     - write(pdf_path, "original.pdf")       # reads from disk → into ZIP
       54 +     - writestr("markups.json", ...)
       55 +     - writestr("layers.json", ...)
       56 +     - writestr("scale.json", ...)
       57 +4. Return Response(buf.getvalue(), media_type="application/zip",
       58 +                   Content-Disposition: "attachment; filename=*.orevu")
       59  ```
       60 +Reuses: `_default_layers()`, `_default_scale()`, existing `db.*` methods.
       61 +Import: add `import io, zipfile` at top (stdlib, no new deps).
       62 +Add `from datetime import datetime` if not already imported (it is — db.py uses it, but main.py needs
          +it here too — check: yes, it's not yet imported in main.py, add it).
       63
       73 ----
       74 -

       80 -| `static/js/layers.js` | NEW — LayerManager class |
       81 -| `static/js/app.js` | Import LayerManager; init in constructor; wire in `_onDocumentLoaded` + markup-
          -list |

       86 -| `test_phase5_layers.mjs` | NEW — 25 tests |
       87 -| `run_tests.mjs` | Append `test_phase5_layers.mjs` to TEST_FILES |

       91 -## Detailed Changes
       92 -
       93 -### 1. canvas.js (line 64) — Add layerId to CUSTOM_PROPERTIES
       94 -
       95 -After `'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints'`:
       96 -```javascript
       97 -    'pairedId', 'lineEndpoints',
       98 -    // Phase 5: layer assignment — which layer this markup belongs to
       99 -    'layerId',
       64 +**POST `/api/documents/import-bundle`**
       65  ```
      101 -No other canvas.js changes. `stampDefaults()` is NOT touched — layerId assignment
      102 -happens in LayerManager's `object:added` hook, keeping canvas.js fully decoupled.
      103 -
      104 ----
      105 -
      106 -### 2. layers.js — NEW
      107 -
      108 -Layer row HTML (built in `refresh()`, content via textContent):
      109 -```html
      110 -<div class="layer-row [active] [layer-hidden] [layer-locked]" data-layer-id="{id}">
      111 -  <button class="layer-vis-btn"  title="Toggle visibility">👁</button>
      112 -  <button class="layer-lock-btn" title="Toggle lock">🔓</button>
      113 -  <span class="layer-name" title="Double-click to rename">{name}</span>
      114 -  <button class="layer-delete-btn" title="Delete layer">×</button>
      115 -</div>
       66 +1. Validate .orevu extension + size (same MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE guard)
       67 +2. zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(content)) — validate it's a real ZIP
       68 +3. Check 'metadata.json' + 'original.pdf' both present
       69 +4. Parse metadata.json → get original filename
       70 +5. Extract 'original.pdf' bytes → magic number check (b'%PDF-')
       71 +6. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR with new uuid filename
       72 +7. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() — validates PDF + gets page_count
       73 +8. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)
       74 +9. If markups.json present → db.save_markups(doc_id, pages)
       75 +10. If layers.json present → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'layers', raw)
       76 +11. If scale.json present  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)
       77 +12. Return JSONResponse({id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}})
       78  ```
      117 -- `active` — blue left border (matches markup-row pattern)
      118 -- `layer-hidden` — dims row + strikethrough name; added when `!layer.visible`
      119 -- `layer-locked` — amber lock icon; added when `layer.locked`
      120 -- Delete `×` appears on hover via CSS opacity
      121 -- Double-click on `.layer-name` → replace span with `<input>`, blur/Enter saves
       79 +Security: same guards as `/api/upload` — extension check, size limit, magic
       80 +number, uuid'd filename, no path traversal. Non-fatal failures for markups/
       81 +layers/scale (logged, fall through to defaults).
       82

      124 -- `_objectAddedHandler` stored on instance so `initForCanvas()` can `fc.off()` it on rebind
      125 -- `deleteLayer()` re-assigns orphaned objects to `'default'` BEFORE splicing the array
      126 -- `load()` is async; `await` it in app.js `_onDocumentLoaded` so layer state is set

      190
      191  ## Gotchas
      192
      367 -- `initForCanvas(fc)` must store handler as `this._objectAddedHandler` and call
      368 -  `fc.off('object:added', this._objectAddedHandler)` before rebinding — Fabric uses
      369 -  named function references for `off()`.
      370 -- `object:added` fires during `loadFromJSON` for objects that already have `layerId`;
      371 -  skip assignment (`if (!obj.layerId)`) but always call `_applyLayerState(obj)`.
      372 -- `load(docId)` is `async` — `await` it in `_onDocumentLoaded` so layer defs are ready
      373 -  before the 200ms setTimeout fires for `_loadMarkups()`.
      374 -- `deleteLayer()` must reassign orphaned objects to `'default'` BEFORE splicing the
      375 -  array, otherwise `_applyAllLayerStates()` can't find the old layer.
      376 -- `_bindTabSwitching()` uses `querySelectorAll('.panel-tab')` at bind time — the Layers
      377 -  tab `<button>` must be in the static HTML so it's captured when `init()` runs.
      378 -- Lock state does NOT hide objects — `visible` stays true, only `selectable` + `evented`
      379 -  go false. Hidden + locked is the intersection of both rules.
      380 -- Export label fix: change `&#128190; Export` → `&#128190; Export PDF` in editor.html.
      193 +- `datetime` is not yet imported in main.py — add to imports (needed for `exported_at`)
      194 +- `zipfile.BadZipFile` must be caught during import; also catch `KeyError` for missing
      195 +  members in metadata parsing
      196 +- On import, `db.save_markups` expects `pages` dict keyed by page number strings
      197 +  (`"0"`, `"1"` etc.) — the bundled `markups.json` already uses this format
      198 +- `zf.write(pdf_path, "original.pdf")` copies the disk file into the ZIP in-memory;
      199 +  for large PDFs this doubles memory temporarily — acceptable for local single-user use
      200 +- The `.orevu` import creates a NEW document entry (never overwrites); the original
      201 +  document on the source machine is untouched
      202 +- `ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` in main.py is `{".pdf"}` — import-bundle does NOT use this
      203 +  constant; it has its own `.orevu` extension check inline
      204 +- Test Group 3 needs to: export bundle via HTTP, decode bytes as ZIP in Node,
      205 +  find `original.pdf` bytes, POST as FormData multipart — use the `node-fetch`-
      206 +  compatible `fetch` + `FormData` available in Node 18+ (same pattern as other tests)
      207 +- Server restart required after adding endpoints (not running with --reload in tests)

       82 +## Phase 1 — Bundle Export/Import (.portolan)
       83 +
       84 +### Bundle Format
       85 +
       86 +`.portolan` is a ZIP (DEFLATED) containing:
       87 +
       88  | File | Contents |
       89  |------|----------|
       30 -| `metadata.json` | `{version, app, filename, page_count, exported_at}` |
       90 +| `metadata.json` | `{version, app:"PortolanCAST", filename, page_count, exported_at}` |
       91  | `original.pdf` | Byte-exact copy of the source PDF |
       92  | `markups.json` | `{pages: {"0": fabricJSON, ...}}` — same as markups API |
       93  | `layers.json` | `{layers:[...], activeId}` — same as layers API |
       94  | `scale.json` | `{preset, paper_inches_per_unit, label}` — same as scale API |
       95
       36 -Only `metadata.json` and `original.pdf` are required; the others are optional
       37 -(import falls back to defaults if absent). This keeps bundles valid even if the
       38 -user exports from a version that predates layers or scale.
       96 +Only `metadata.json` + `original.pdf` required; others are optional (import falls
       97 +back to defaults). This ensures forward/backward compatibility.

      130 +Validation:

      132 +  - PDF bytes must start with b'%PDF-'
      133 +
      134 +Restore sequence:
      135 +  1. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR (uuid filename)
      136 +  2. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() to validate + get page_count
      137 +  3. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)
      138 +  4. If markups.json → db.save_markups(doc_id, pages)
      139 +  5. If layers.json  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'layers', raw)
      140 +  6. If scale.json   → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)
      141 +
      142 +Return: {id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}}

       66 -1. Validate .orevu extension + size (same MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE guard)

       69 -4. Parse metadata.json → get original filename
       70 -5. Extract 'original.pdf' bytes → magic number check (b'%PDF-')
       71 -6. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR with new uuid filename
       72 -7. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() — validates PDF + gets page_count
       73 -8. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)

       76 -11. If scale.json present  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)
       77 -12. Return JSONResponse({id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}})

╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 PortolanCAST — Rename + Bundle Export

 Date: 2026-02-24
 Goals:
 1. Rename the project from "OpenRevu" to "PortolanCAST" across all source files
 2. Add a portable save format (.portolan ZIP bundle) for full re-editing across machines
 3. Write MechDraw AI plugin spec to PKM vault (spec only, not implemented)

 ---
 Context

 The project has outgrown its working name. "OpenRevu" implied a clone of Bluebeam
 Revu; "PortolanCAST" is original — portolan charts were the first precise technical
 navigation maps (1200s–1600s), a fitting metaphor for a tool that annotates and
 measures technical drawings. CAST = Computer-Aided Systems Thinking.

 Bundle export solves the "share-for-review" workflow: export a .portolan ZIP that
 contains the original PDF plus all markups, layers, and scale settings. The recipient
 opens it like any PDF — the full session is restored exactly.

 ---
 Phase 0 — Rename

 Scope (source files only — venv/ and node_modules/ excluded)

 String replacements:
 - "OpenRevu" → "PortolanCAST" (title-case brand name)
 - "openrevu" → "portolancast" (lowercase, used in DB path and status messages)
 - "OpenRevu Team" → "PortolanCAST" (author field in file headers)
 - "openrevu.db" → "portolancast.db" (database filename in db.py)

 Files with source changes (venv/ and node_modules/ skipped):
 - main.py (4 occurrences — app title, comments)
 - db.py (5 — DEFAULT_DB_PATH uses "openrevu.db", docstring)
 - pdf_engine.py (2 — docstring)
 - spike_calibration.py (4 — header/comments)
 - requirements.txt (1 — comment)
 - run.sh (2 — banner/comment)
 - QUICKSTART.md (3 — references)
 - templates/editor.html (3 — title tag, console banners)
 - templates/base.html (1 — title)
 - static/css/style.css (1 — comment)
 - static/js/app.js (5 — docstring, console logs)
 - static/js/toolbar.js (8 — docstring, console logs)
 - static/js/canvas.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/pdf-viewer.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/properties.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/markup-list.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/scale.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/plugins.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/layers.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/measure.js (3 — docstring)
 - static/js/measure-summary.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/node-editor.js (2 — docstring)
 - static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js (3 — docstring)
 - run_tests.mjs (4 — banner, comments)
 - All test_*.mjs files (2–8 each — docstring + banner)

 DB path migration note

 db.py line 30: DEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "data" / "openrevu.db"
 → change to portolancast.db.

 Existing local data: the old openrevu.db will no longer be found automatically.
 If the user has existing documents they want to keep, they should rename the file
 manually: mv data/openrevu.db data/portolancast.db. Add a note in QUICKSTART.md.

 Implementation strategy

 Use Bash sed -i for bulk replacement across source files (fastest approach).
 Run full test suite after rename to confirm nothing broke.

 # Example (run per-file or with find):
 sed -i 's/OpenRevu/PortolanCAST/g; s/openrevu/portolancast/g' main.py db.py ...

 ---
 Phase 1 — Bundle Export/Import (.portolan)

 Bundle Format

 .portolan is a ZIP (DEFLATED) containing:

 ┌───────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │     File      │                             Contents                             │
 ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ metadata.json │ {version, app:"PortolanCAST", filename, page_count, exported_at} │
 ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ original.pdf  │ Byte-exact copy of the source PDF                                │
 ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ markups.json  │ {pages: {"0": fabricJSON, ...}} — same as markups API            │
 ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ layers.json   │ {layers:[...], activeId} — same as layers API                    │
 ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ scale.json    │ {preset, paper_inches_per_unit, label} — same as scale API       │
 └───────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Only metadata.json + original.pdf required; others are optional (import falls
 back to defaults). This ensures forward/backward compatibility.

 Backend (main.py)

 New imports (add to existing imports at top):
 import io
 import zipfile
 from datetime import datetime

 GET /api/documents/{doc_id}/export-bundle:
 1. Verify doc + PDF file exist
 2. Load markups (db.get_markups), layers (db.get_document_setting('layers')),
 scale (db.get_document_setting('scale'))
 3. Build in-memory zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(), 'w', ZIP_DEFLATED):
   - writestr("metadata.json", ...) with datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
   - zf.write(str(pdf_path), "original.pdf") — reads PDF from disk into ZIP
   - writestr("markups.json", json.dumps({pages}))
   - writestr("layers.json", ...) — raw setting or _default_layers()
   - writestr("scale.json", ...) — raw setting or _default_scale()
 4. return Response(buf.getvalue(), media_type="application/zip",             headers={"Content-Disposition":
 'attachment; filename="X.portolan"'})

 Download filename: strip .pdf from doc["filename"], append .portolan.

 POST /api/documents/import-bundle:
 Security checks (same pattern as /api/upload):
   - Extension must be .portolan
   - Size <= MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE
   - zipfile.BadZipFile caught → 400

 Validation:
   - 'metadata.json' and 'original.pdf' must be in namelist()
   - PDF bytes must start with b'%PDF-'

 Restore sequence:
   1. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR (uuid filename)
   2. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() to validate + get page_count
   3. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)
   4. If markups.json → db.save_markups(doc_id, pages)
   5. If layers.json  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'layers', raw)
   6. If scale.json   → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)

 Return: {id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}}

 Frontend (toolbar.js)

 _handleBundleSave() — mirrors _handleExport():
 1. Guard !this.viewer.docId → alert
 2. Save markups (same save-first pattern as export)
 3. fetch(export-bundle) → blob → temp <a> download (Content-Disposition filename)

 _handleBundleImport(file) — mirrors _handleFileUpload():
 4. POST FormData to /api/documents/import-bundle
 5. history.pushState + viewer.loadDocument(result.id)

 Extend _handleFileUpload() (add at very top of method):
 if (file.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.portolan')) {
     return this._handleBundleImport(file);
 }
 Change the old PDF-only alert to: 'Please select a PDF or .portolan bundle file.'

 _bindButtons() addition (after export button binding):
 const btnSaveBundle = document.getElementById('btn-save-bundle');
 if (btnSaveBundle) {
     btnSaveBundle.addEventListener('click', () => this._handleBundleSave());
 }

 HTML (editor.html)

 Add to .toolbar-export div (after Export PDF button):
 <button id="btn-save-bundle" class="toolbar-btn"
         title="Save editable bundle (.portolan) — preserves markups and layers for re-editing">
     <span class="icon">&#128230;</span> Save Bundle
 </button>

 Extend #file-input accept:
 <input type="file" id="file-input" accept=".pdf,.portolan" style="display: none;">

 Files Changed (Phase 1)

 ┌───────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │         File          │                                         Change                                         │
 ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ main.py               │ Add io, zipfile, datetime imports; GET export-bundle; POST import-bundle               │
 ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ static/js/toolbar.js  │ _handleBundleSave(), _handleBundleImport(), extend _handleFileUpload(), _bindButtons() │
 ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ templates/editor.html │ #btn-save-bundle button; extend file-input accept                                      │
 ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ test_bundle.mjs       │ NEW — 10 tests                                                                         │
 ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
 │ run_tests.mjs         │ Append test_bundle.mjs                                                                 │
 └───────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ---
 Test Plan — test_bundle.mjs (10 tests)

 Group 1: UI Shell (2)
 - #btn-save-bundle exists inside .toolbar-export
 - #file-input accept attribute contains .portolan

 Group 2: Export Bundle Endpoint (5)
 - GET /api/documents/1/export-bundle → HTTP 200
 - Content-Type header is application/zip
 - Content-Disposition contains .portolan filename
 - Extracted ZIP namelist contains all 5 required files
 - metadata.json has version, filename, page_count fields

 Group 3: Import Round-trip (3)
 - POST exported bundle to /api/documents/import-bundle → 200 with id
 - GET /edit/{id} → 200 (imported doc accessible)
 - Imported doc page_count matches original

 Target after both phases: 464 + 10 = 474 passing

 ---
 Phase 2 — MechDraw AI Plugin Spec (PKM only)

 After Phase 1 tests pass, write spec to:
 /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/PKM/Inbox/MechDraw_Plugin_Spec_2_24_2026.md

 Covers:
 - Problem: natural language → duct layout (HVAC/mechanical)
 - Input modes: text prompt + optional floor plan image (vision via Ollama)
 - Output: Fabric.js objects (Path/Group) injected onto canvas via PluginLoader
 - Integration: plugins.register('mechdraw', {...}) using existing lifecycle hooks
 - Deferred: marked "Phase X — Future" in PIPELINE.md

 ---
 Implementation Order

 1. Phase 0 — Rename: sed across all source files (exclude venv/, node_modules/)
   - Update DEFAULT_DB_PATH in db.py: openrevu.db → portolancast.db
   - Add migration note to QUICKSTART.md
   - Restart server + run full test suite (verify 464/464 still pass)
 2. Phase 1 — Bundle: main.py endpoints → toolbar.js → editor.html → tests
   - Restart server + run full suite (target 474/474)
 3. Phase 2 — Spec: write MechDraw plugin spec markdown to PKM

 - .portolan import creates a NEW doc (never overwrites existing)

│ 2. Load markups (db.get_markups), layers (db.get_document_setting('layers')),                                        │

│   - writestr("scale.json", ...) — raw setting or _default_scale()                                                    │
│ 4. return Response(buf.getvalue(), media_type="application/zip",             headers={"Content-Disposition":         │
│ 'attachment; filename="X.portolan"'})                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Download filename: strip .pdf from doc["filename"], append .portolan.                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ POST /api/documents/import-bundle:                                                                                   │
│ Security checks (same pattern as /api/upload):                                                                       │
│   - Extension must be .portolan                                                                                      │
│   - Size <= MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE                                                                                          │
│   - zipfile.BadZipFile caught → 400                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Validation:                                                                                                          │
│   - 'metadata.json' and 'original.pdf' must be in namelist()                                                         │
│   - PDF bytes must start with b'%PDF-'                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Restore sequence:                                                                                                    │
│   1. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR (uuid filename)                                                                        │
│   2. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() to validate + get page_count                                                          │
│   3. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)                                                      │
│   4. If markups.json → db.save_markups(doc_id, pages)                                                                │
│   5. If layers.json  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'layers', raw)                                                │
│   6. If scale.json   → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Return: {id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}}                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Frontend (toolbar.js)                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _handleBundleSave() — mirrors _handleExport():                                                                       │
│ 1. Guard !this.viewer.docId → alert                                                                                  │
│ 2. Save markups (same save-first pattern as export)                                                                  │
│ 3. fetch(export-bundle) → blob → temp <a> download (Content-Disposition filename)                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _handleBundleImport(file) — mirrors _handleFileUpload():                                                             │
│ 1. POST FormData to /api/documents/import-bundle                                                                     │
│ 2. history.pushState + viewer.loadDocument(result.id)                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Extend _handleFileUpload() (add at very top of method):                                                              │
│ if (file.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.portolan')) {                                                                 │
│     return this._handleBundleImport(file);                                                                           │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│ Change the old PDF-only alert to: 'Please select a PDF or .portolan bundle file.'                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _bindButtons() addition (after export button binding):                                                               │
│ const btnSaveBundle = document.getElementById('btn-save-bundle');                                                    │
│ if (btnSaveBundle) {                                                                                                 │
│     btnSaveBundle.addEventListener('click', () => this._handleBundleSave());                                         │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ HTML (editor.html)                                                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Add to .toolbar-export div (after Export PDF button):                                                                │
│ <button id="btn-save-bundle" class="toolbar-btn"                                                                     │
│         title="Save editable bundle (.portolan) — preserves markups and layers for re-editing">                      │
│     <span class="icon">&#128230;</span> Save Bundle                                                                  │
│ </button>                                                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Extend #file-input accept:                                                                                           │
│ <input type="file" id="file-input" accept=".pdf,.portolan" style="display: none;">                                   │

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The rename phase uses sed -i for bulk substitution — faster than reading+editing each file individually in Claude. The
   key is excluding venv/ and node_modules/ since those are third-party packages where our brand name doesn't belong. We
   also need to handle three case variants: OpenRevu (title), openrevu (lowercase), and OpenRevu Team (author) as
  distinct patterns.

         </div>

         <!-- Mode tabs: Navigate | Markup | Measure
              Tab buttons sit inline in the toolbar bar — no second row.
              Clicking a tab shows its tool panel and hides the others. -->
         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-tabs">
             <button class="toolbar-tab active" data-tab="navigate">Navigate</button>
             <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="markup">Markup</button>
             <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="measure">Measure</button>
         </div>

         <!-- Navigate tab: pan and select tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="navigate">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <!-- Hand tool: pan mode — events pass through canvas so viewport scrolls -->
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="hand" title="Hand / Pan (G)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9995;</span> Hand
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="select" title="Select (V)">
                     <span class="icon">&#8630;</span> Select
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Markup tab: annotation drawing tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="markup" style="display:none">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="pen" title="Pen (P)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9999;&#65039;</span> Pen
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="rect" title="Rectangle (R)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9645;</span> Rect
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="ellipse" title="Ellipse (E)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9711;</span> Ellipse
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="line" title="Line (L)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9585;</span> Line
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="highlighter" title="Highlighter (H)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9998;</span> Highlight
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="text" title="Text (T)">
                     <span class="icon">T</span> Text
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="cloud" title="Cloud (C)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9729;</span> Cloud
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="callout" title="Callout (O)">
                     <span class="icon">&#128172;</span> Callout
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Measure tab: takeoff and calibration tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="measure" style="display:none">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="distance" title="Distance Ruler (U)">
                     <span class="icon">&#128207;</span> Distance
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="area" title="Area Polygon (A)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9649;</span> Area
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="count" title="Count Markers (N)">
                     <span class="icon">&#35;</span> Count
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="calibrate" title="Calibrate Scale (K)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9878;</span> Calibrate
                 </button>
                 <!-- Node Edit: enter vertex/endpoint edit mode for selected measurement -->
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="node-edit" title="Node Edit — vertex/endpoint
     handles for selected measurement">
                     <span class="icon">&#11835;</span> Node Edit
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Settings gear — always visible, not inside any tab panel -->
         <button id="btn-toolbar-settings" class="toolbar-btn toolbar-settings-btn" title="Toolbar settings —
     show/hide tools">&#9881;</button>

         <!-- Intent mode indicator — shows active markup type -->
         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-intent">
             <span id="intent-indicator" class="intent-indicator" title="Active markup type (1-5 to change)">
                 <span id="intent-dot" class="intent-dot"></span>
                 <span id="intent-label">Note</span>
             </span>
         </div>

         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-export">
             <button id="btn-export" class="toolbar-btn"
                     title="Export annotated PDF — markups become permanent (not editable after export)">
                 <span class="icon">&#128190;</span> Export PDF
             </button>
         </div>

         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-zoom">
             <button id="btn-zoom-out" class="toolbar-btn" title="Zoom Out (-)">−</button>
             <span id="zoom-display" class="zoom-display">100%</span>
             <button id="btn-zoom-in" class="toolbar-btn" title="Zoom In (+)">+</button>
             <button id="btn-zoom-fit" class="toolbar-btn" title="Fit to Width">Fit</button>
         </div>
     </div>

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- MAIN CONTENT AREA                                                       -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="main-content">

         <!-- LEFT PANEL: Pages + Markups (tabbed) -->
         <div id="panel-left" class="side-panel">
             <div class="panel-tabs">
                 <button class="panel-tab active" data-panel="pages">Pages</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="markups">Markups</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="measures">Measures</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="layers">Layers</button>
             </div>

             <!-- Pages tab content -->
             <div id="tab-pages" class="tab-content active">
                 <div id="thumbnail-list" class="thumbnail-list">
                     <!-- Thumbnails inserted dynamically -->
                 </div>
                 <!-- Add blank page — only shown when a document is open -->
                 <div id="pages-panel-footer" class="pages-panel-footer" style="display: none;">
                     <button id="btn-add-page" class="btn-add-page" title="Append a blank page to this document">
                         + Add Page
                     </button>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Markups tab content -->
             <div id="tab-markups" class="tab-content">
                 <!-- Summary bar: count + filter -->
                 <div id="markup-list-header">
                     <span id="markup-count">0 markups</span>
                     <select id="markup-filter" title="Filter by type">
                         <option value="all">All Types</option>
                         <optgroup label="Markups">
                             <option value="note">Note</option>
                             <option value="issue">Issue</option>
                             <option value="question">Question</option>
                             <option value="approval">Approval</option>
                             <option value="change">Change</option>
                         </optgroup>
                         <optgroup label="Measurements">
                             <option value="distance">Distance</option>
                             <option value="area">Area</option>
                             <option value="count">Count</option>
                         </optgroup>
                     </select>
                     <select id="markup-status-filter" title="Filter by status">
                         <option value="all">All Status</option>
                         <option value="open">Open</option>
                         <option value="resolved">Resolved</option>
                     </select>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Scrollable markup rows -->
                 <div id="markup-list" class="markup-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="markup-list-empty">No markups yet</p>
                     <!-- Rows inserted dynamically by markup-list.js -->
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Layers tab content — Phase 5 layer management -->
             <div id="tab-layers" class="tab-content">
                 <div id="layers-header">
                     <span id="layers-count">0 layers</span>
                     <button id="btn-new-layer" class="toolbar-btn layers-new-btn"
                             title="Add a new layer">+ Layer</button>
                 </div>
                 <div id="layers-list" class="layers-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="layers-empty">No layers</p>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Measures tab content — Phase 4A measurement summary panel -->
             <div id="tab-measures" class="tab-content">
                 <!-- Stat cards: one per measurement type -->
                 <div id="measure-stats" class="measure-stats">
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-distance-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Distances</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-distance-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-area-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Areas</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-area-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-count-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Count Items</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-count-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Filter row + export button -->
                 <div id="measure-list-header">
                     <select id="measure-type-filter">
                         <option value="all">All Types</option>
                         <option value="distance">Distance</option>
                         <option value="area">Area</option>
                         <option value="count">Count</option>
                     </select>
                     <button id="btn-export-csv" class="toolbar-btn measure-export-btn"
                             title="Export measurements to CSV">Export CSV</button>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Scrollable measurement rows — inserted dynamically by measure-summary.js -->
                 <div id="measure-list" class="markup-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="measure-list-empty">No measurements yet</p>
                 </div>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- CENTER: PDF Canvas viewport -->
         <div id="viewport">
             <!-- Welcome screen (shown when no document is open) -->
             <div id="welcome-screen">
                 <div class="welcome-content">
                     <h1>PortolanCAST</h1>
                     <p>Open-source PDF markup &amp; measurement</p>
                     <button id="btn-welcome-open" class="welcome-btn">Open a PDF</button>


                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Canvas container (hidden until a PDF is loaded) -->
             <div id="canvas-container" style="display: none;">
                 <!-- PDF rendered image -->
                 <img id="pdf-image" alt="PDF page" draggable="false">
                 <!-- Fabric.js markup overlay — positioned absolutely over pdf-image via CSS -->
                 <canvas id="fabric-canvas"></canvas>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- RIGHT PANEL: Properties / Plugin space -->
         <div id="panel-properties" class="side-panel">
             <!-- Tab bar — Properties always present; plugin tabs injected by PluginLoader.register() -->
             <div class="panel-tabs" id="right-panel-tabs">
                 <button class="panel-tab active" data-panel="properties">Properties</button>
             </div>

             <!-- Properties tab — wraps existing content unchanged -->
             <div id="tab-properties" class="tab-content active">
             <div id="properties-content" class="panel-body">

                 <!-- Measurement properties — shown when a measurement object is selected -->
                 <!-- Hidden by default; shown instead of #markup-semantic-props for measurements -->
                 <div id="measurement-props" style="display: none;">
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Measurement</div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label id="measure-label">Value:</label>
                         <span id="measure-value" class="measure-value-large">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <!-- Secondary row: total count (count tool) or pixel value (all tools) -->
                     <div class="prop-row" id="measure-secondary-row" style="display: none;">
                         <label id="measure-secondary-label">Total:</label>
                         <span id="measure-secondary-value">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Scale:</label>
                         <span id="measure-scale-label" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>

                 <!-- Markup properties — shown when a markup object is selected -->
                 <div id="markup-props" style="display: none;">
                     <!-- Semantic fields — hidden for measurement objects -->
                     <div id="markup-semantic-props">
                         <div class="prop-section-header">Markup</div>
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Object:</label>
                             <span id="prop-obj-type">—</span>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-type">Type</label>
                             <select id="prop-markup-type">
                                 <option value="note">Note</option>
                                 <option value="issue">Issue</option>
                                 <option value="question">Question</option>
                                 <option value="approval">Approval</option>
                                 <option value="change">Change</option>
                             </select>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-status">Status</label>
                             <select id="prop-markup-status">
                                 <option value="open">Open</option>
                                 <option value="resolved">Resolved</option>
                             </select>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-note">Note</label>
                             <textarea id="prop-markup-note" rows="3"
                                       placeholder="Add a note..."></textarea>
                         </div>
                         <!-- Read-only provenance — who made it, when -->
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Author:</label>
                             <span id="prop-markup-author" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Created:</label>
                             <span id="prop-markup-timestamp" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                         </div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Appearance</div>
                     <div class="prop-field">
                         <label for="prop-stroke-color">Color</label>
                         <input type="color" id="prop-stroke-color" value="#ff0000">
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-field">
                         <label for="prop-stroke-width">Width</label>
                         <input type="range" id="prop-stroke-width"
                                min="1" max="10" value="2" step="1">
                         <span id="prop-stroke-width-val" class="range-val">2</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>

                 <!-- Document info — shown when no markup is selected -->
                 <div id="doc-info" style="display: none;">
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Document</div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>File:</label>
                         <span id="prop-filename">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Pages:</label>
                         <span id="prop-pages">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Size:</label>
                         <span id="prop-size">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Page Dims:</label>
                         <span id="prop-dims">—</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <p id="no-doc-msg" class="muted-text">Open a PDF to see properties</p>
             </div>
             </div>
             <!-- Plugin tab content divs injected here by PluginLoader._injectTab() -->
         </div>
     </div>

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- BOTTOM STATUS BAR                                                       -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="status-bar">
         <span id="status-page">Page: — / —</span>
         <span id="status-zoom">Zoom: 100%</span>
         <!-- Drawing scale selector — set to match the title block scale on the drawing -->
         <span id="status-scale" class="status-scale" style="display: none;" title="Drawing scale — set to match
     the title block">
             <label for="scale-select" class="scale-prefix">Scale:</label>
             <!-- scale-label: shown as text when a preset is active (read by _updateStatusBar) -->
             <span id="scale-label" class="scale-label-text" style="display:none;"></span>
             <select id="scale-select" class="scale-select-input">
                 <option value="unscaled">Unscaled</option>
                 <!-- Hidden option for programmatic selection when calibration sets custom scale -->
                 <option value="custom" style="display:none;">Custom</option>
                 <optgroup label="Architectural">
                     <option value="arch_3_32">3/32" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_8">1/8" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_16">3/16" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_4">1/4" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_8">3/8" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_2">1/2" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_4">3/4" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_1">1" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_half">1-1/2" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_1">3" = 1'</option>
                 </optgroup>
                 <optgroup label="Engineering">
                     <option value="eng_1_10">1" = 10'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_20">1" = 20'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_30">1" = 30'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_40">1" = 40'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_50">1" = 50'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_60">1" = 60'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_100">1" = 100'</option>
                 </optgroup>
             </select>
         </span>
         <span id="status-counts" class="status-counts" style="display: none;">
             <span class="status-count-item count-issue" title="Issues">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-question" title="Questions">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-approval" title="Approvals">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-change" title="Changes">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-sep">|</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-open" title="Open markups">0 open</span>
         </span>
         <span id="status-message"></span>
     </div>

     <!-- Hidden file input for PDF upload -->
     <input type="file" id="file-input" accept=".pdf" style="display: none;">

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- NEW DOCUMENT MODAL                                                      -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-new-doc" class="modal" style="display: none;" role="dialog" aria-modal="true"
     aria-labelledby="modal-new-doc-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-new-doc-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box">
             <h2 id="modal-new-doc-title">New Document</h2>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-name">Name</label>
                 <input type="text" id="new-doc-name" value="Untitled" maxlength="120"
                        placeholder="Document name" autocomplete="off">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-pages">Pages</label>
                 <input type="number" id="new-doc-pages" value="1" min="1" max="50">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-size">Page Size</label>
                 <select id="new-doc-size">
                     <option value="letter">Letter (8.5" × 11")</option>
                     <option value="tabloid">Tabloid (11" × 17")</option>
                     <option value="arch_c">Arch C (18" × 24")</option>
                     <option value="arch_d">Arch D (24" × 36")</option>
                     <option value="arch_e">Arch E (36" × 48")</option>
                     <option value="a4">A4 (8.3" × 11.7")</option>
                     <option value="legal">Legal (8.5" × 14")</option>
                 </select>
             </div>
             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="new-doc-cancel" class="toolbar-btn">Cancel</button>
                 <button id="new-doc-create" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Create</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- CALIBRATION MODAL                                                       -->
     <!-- Shown after the user draws a reference line with the Calibrate tool.   -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-calibrate" class="modal" style="display: none;"
          role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="modal-calibrate-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-calibrate-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box">
             <h2 id="modal-calibrate-title">Calibrate Scale</h2>
             <p class="modal-hint">Draw a line over a known dimension, then enter its real length below.</p>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="calib-pixels">Measured Length (pixels)</label>
                 <input type="text" id="calib-pixels" readonly
                        placeholder="—" style="opacity: 0.7; cursor: default;">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="calib-value">Real-World Length</label>
                 <div class="calib-input-row">
                     <input type="number" id="calib-value" min="0.01" step="any"
                            placeholder="e.g. 20" style="flex: 1;">
                     <select id="calib-unit" style="flex: 0 0 auto; width: 100px;">
                         <option value="ft">feet</option>
                         <option value="in">inches</option>
                         <option value="m">meters</option>
                         <option value="cm">centimeters</option>
                     </select>
                 </div>
             </div>
             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="calib-cancel" class="toolbar-btn">Cancel</button>
                 <button id="calib-apply" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Apply Scale</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- TOOLBAR SETTINGS MODAL                                              -->
     <!-- Allows per-tool show/hide with checkboxes, persisted in localStorage-->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-toolbar-settings" class="modal" style="display: none;"
          role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="modal-settings-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-settings-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box modal-settings-box">
             <h2 id="modal-settings-title">Toolbar Settings</h2>
             <p class="modal-hint">Choose which tools appear in each tab.</p>

             <div class="settings-tab-sections">
                 <!-- Navigate tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Navigate</div>
                     <div id="settings-navigate-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically by toolbar.js -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Markup tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Markup</div>
                     <div id="settings-markup-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Measure tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Measure</div>
                     <div id="settings-measure-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="settings-reset" class="toolbar-btn">Reset to Default</button>
                 <button id="settings-close" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Done</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>


     <!-- Fabric.js 6.9.1 — UMD bundle, sets globalThis.fabric -->
     <script src="/static/lib/fabric.min.js"></script>
     <!-- Main application (ES module) — loads after Fabric.js is available -->
     <script src="/static/js/app.js" type="module"></script>

     </body>
     </html>

● Now Phase 1: Bundle Export/Import. Let me read the relevant files first:

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The .portolan ZIP format uses Python's io.BytesIO() as an in-memory file buffer — no temp file needed. zipfile.ZipFile
   writes directly into the BytesIO, then buf.getvalue() extracts the bytes for the HTTP response. This is faster and
  safer than writing to disk. The ZIP_DEFLATED compression typically achieves 60-80% size reduction for text JSON
  payloads (markups, layers, scale) while leaving the PDF mostly unchanged (PDFs are already compressed).
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/main.py)
  ⎿  Added 228 lines
      1090
      1091
      1092  # =============================================================================
      1093 +# API ROUTES — BUNDLE EXPORT/IMPORT (.portolan)
      1094 +# =============================================================================
      1095 +
      1096 +@app.get("/api/documents/{doc_id}/export-bundle")
      1097 +async def export_bundle(doc_id: int):
      1098 +    """
      1099 +    Export a portable .portolan bundle for a document.
      1100 +
      1101 +    A .portolan file is a ZIP archive containing the original PDF, all markup
      1102 +    data, layer configuration, and scale settings. Recipients can import the
      1103 +    bundle to restore the full session — PDF + markups + layers — on any
      1104 +    PortolanCAST installation without needing access to the original file.
      1105 +
      1106 +    Bundle contents:
      1107 +        metadata.json  — app version, filename, page count, export timestamp
      1108 +        original.pdf   — byte-exact copy of the source PDF
      1109 +        markups.json   — { pages: { "0": fabricJSON, ... } }
      1110 +        layers.json    — { layers: [...], activeId }
      1111 +        scale.json     — { preset, paper_inches_per_unit, unit_label }
      1112 +
      1113 +    Security:
      1114 +        - Validates document + PDF file exist before building the bundle
      1115 +        - In-memory ZIP — no temp files written to disk
      1116 +        - Markup/layer/scale data is read from DB, never from request body
      1117 +
      1118 +    Args:
      1119 +        doc_id: Document database ID.
      1120 +
      1121 +    Returns:
      1122 +        ZIP file as application/zip with Content-Disposition attachment.
      1123 +    """
      1124 +    doc = db.get_document(doc_id)
      1125 +    if not doc:
      1126 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Document not found")
      1127 +
      1128 +    pdf_path = Path(doc["filepath"])
      1129 +    if not pdf_path.exists():
      1130 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="PDF file missing from disk")
      1131 +
      1132 +    # Load all document state from DB
      1133 +    markups = db.get_markups(doc_id)
      1134 +    pages_str = {str(k): v for k, v in markups.items()}
      1135 +
      1136 +    raw_layers = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "layers")
      1137 +    if raw_layers:
      1138 +        try:
      1139 +            layers_obj = _json_top.loads(raw_layers)
      1140 +        except Exception:
      1141 +            layers_obj = _default_layers()
      1142 +    else:
      1143 +        layers_obj = _default_layers()
      1144 +
      1145 +    raw_scale = db.get_document_setting(doc_id, "scale")
      1146 +    if raw_scale:
      1147 +        try:
      1148 +            scale_obj = _json_top.loads(raw_scale)
      1149 +        except Exception:
      1150 +            scale_obj = _default_scale()
      1151 +    else:
      1152 +        scale_obj = _default_scale()
      1153 +
      1154 +    metadata = {
      1155 +        "version": "1",
      1156 +        "app": "PortolanCAST",
      1157 +        "filename": doc["filename"],
      1158 +        "page_count": doc["page_count"],
      1159 +        "exported_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
      1160 +    }
      1161 +
      1162 +    # Build ZIP in memory — avoids writing temp files to disk
      1163 +    buf = io.BytesIO()
      1164 +    with zipfile.ZipFile(buf, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:
      1165 +        zf.writestr("metadata.json", _json_top.dumps(metadata, indent=2))
      1166 +        zf.write(str(pdf_path), "original.pdf")
      1167 +        zf.writestr("markups.json", _json_top.dumps({"pages": pages_str}, indent=2))
      1168 +        zf.writestr("layers.json", _json_top.dumps(layers_obj, indent=2))
      1169 +        zf.writestr("scale.json", _json_top.dumps(scale_obj, indent=2))
      1170 +
      1171 +    # Build download filename: strip .pdf extension, add .portolan
      1172 +    base_name = doc["filename"]
      1173 +    if base_name.lower().endswith(".pdf"):
      1174 +        base_name = base_name[:-4]
      1175 +    download_name = base_name + ".portolan"
      1176 +
      1177 +    return Response(
      1178 +        content=buf.getvalue(),
      1179 +        media_type="application/zip",
      1180 +        headers={
      1181 +            "Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{download_name}"',
      1182 +        },
      1183 +    )
      1184 +
      1185 +
      1186 +@app.post("/api/documents/import-bundle")
      1187 +async def import_bundle(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
      1188 +    """
      1189 +    Import a .portolan bundle and restore the document + all markup state.
      1190 +
      1191 +    Reads the ZIP, validates required members, saves the PDF, creates a new
      1192 +    document record, and restores markups/layers/scale from the bundle.
      1193 +    Always creates a NEW document — never overwrites an existing one.
      1194 +
      1195 +    Expected bundle (minimum required):
      1196 +        metadata.json  — must be present (version check future-proofing)
      1197 +        original.pdf   — PDF bytes; must start with %PDF- magic bytes
      1198 +
      1199 +    Optional (imported if present, defaults used if absent):
      1200 +        markups.json, layers.json, scale.json
      1201 +
      1202 +    Security:
      1203 +        - Extension must be .portolan
      1204 +        - Size limited by MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE constant
      1205 +        - zipfile.BadZipFile caught to reject corrupt or spoofed ZIPs
      1206 +        - PDF magic bytes validated before saving
      1207 +        - Optional members are schema-validated before DB write
      1208 +
      1209 +    Args:
      1210 +        file: The uploaded .portolan bundle (multipart form).
      1211 +
      1212 +    Returns:
      1213 +        { id, filename, page_count, redirect }
      1214 +    """
      1215 +    # SECURITY: extension check — only .portolan bundles accepted here
      1216 +    if not file.filename or not file.filename.lower().endswith(".portolan"):
      1217 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="File must be a .portolan bundle")
      1218 +
      1219 +    raw = await file.read()
      1220 +
      1221 +    # SECURITY: size limit
      1222 +    if len(raw) > MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE:
      1223 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=413, detail="Bundle exceeds maximum upload size")
      1224 +
      1225 +    # SECURITY: reject corrupt or non-ZIP files
      1226 +    try:
      1227 +        with zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(raw)) as zf:
      1228 +            namelist = zf.namelist()
      1229 +
      1230 +            # Required members
      1231 +            if "metadata.json" not in namelist:
      1232 +                raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Bundle missing metadata.json")
      1233 +            if "original.pdf" not in namelist:
      1234 +                raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Bundle missing original.pdf")
      1235 +
      1236 +            pdf_bytes = zf.read("original.pdf")
      1237 +
      1238 +            # SECURITY: validate PDF magic bytes
      1239 +            if not pdf_bytes.startswith(b"%PDF-"):
      1240 +                raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Bundle contains invalid PDF")
      1241 +
      1242 +            # Read metadata to get original filename
      1243 +            try:
      1244 +                meta = _json_top.loads(zf.read("metadata.json"))
      1245 +            except Exception:
      1246 +                meta = {}
      1247 +            original_filename = str(meta.get("filename", file.filename))
      1248 +
      1249 +            # Read optional members — use defaults if absent
      1250 +            markups_pages = {}
      1251 +            if "markups.json" in namelist:
      1252 +                try:
      1253 +                    markups_data = _json_top.loads(zf.read("markups.json"))
      1254 +                    markups_pages = markups_data.get("pages", {})
      1255 +                    if not isinstance(markups_pages, dict):
      1256 +                        markups_pages = {}
      1257 +                except Exception:
      1258 +                    markups_pages = {}
      1259 +
      1260 +            layers_raw = None
      1261 +            if "layers.json" in namelist:
      1262 +                try:
      1263 +                    layers_obj = _json_top.loads(zf.read("layers.json"))
      1264 +                    if isinstance(layers_obj, dict) and "layers" in layers_obj:
      1265 +                        layers_raw = _json_top.dumps(layers_obj)
      1266 +                except Exception:
      1267 +                    pass
      1268 +
      1269 +            scale_raw = None
      1270 +            if "scale.json" in namelist:
      1271 +                try:
      1272 +                    scale_obj = _json_top.loads(zf.read("scale.json"))
      1273 +                    if isinstance(scale_obj, dict) and "preset" in scale_obj:
      1274 +                        scale_raw = _json_top.dumps(scale_obj)
      1275 +                except Exception:
      1276 +                    pass
      1277 +
      1278 +    except HTTPException:
      1279 +        raise  # re-raise our own validation errors
      1280 +    except zipfile.BadZipFile:
      1281 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="File is not a valid ZIP/portolan bundle")
      1282 +    except Exception as e:
      1283 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Bundle read error: {e}")
      1284 +
      1285 +    # Save PDF to projects directory with a unique filename
      1286 +    pdf_filename = uuid.uuid4().hex + ".pdf"
      1287 +    pdf_dest = PROJECTS_DIR / pdf_filename
      1288 +    PROJECTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      1289 +    pdf_dest.write_bytes(pdf_bytes)
      1290 +
      1291 +    # Validate + measure the PDF
      1292 +    try:
      1293 +        pdf_info = pdf_engine.get_pdf_info(str(pdf_dest))
      1294 +    except Exception as e:
      1295 +        pdf_dest.unlink(missing_ok=True)
      1296 +        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"PDF validation failed: {e}")
      1297 +
      1298 +    file_size = len(pdf_bytes)
      1299 +    page_count = pdf_info.get("page_count", 1)
      1300 +
      1301 +    # Create document record in DB
      1302 +    new_doc_id = db.add_document(original_filename, str(pdf_dest), page_count, file_size)
      1303 +
      1304 +    # Restore optional state
      1305 +    if markups_pages:
      1306 +        db.save_markups(new_doc_id, markups_pages)
      1307 +    if layers_raw:
      1308 +        db.set_document_setting(new_doc_id, "layers", layers_raw)
      1309 +    if scale_raw:
      1310 +        db.set_document_setting(new_doc_id, "scale", scale_raw)
      1311 +
      1312 +    return JSONResponse({
      1313 +        "id": new_doc_id,
      1314 +        "filename": original_filename,
      1315 +        "page_count": page_count,
      1316 +        "redirect": f"/edit/{new_doc_id}",
      1317 +    })
      1318 +
      1319 +
      1320 +# =============================================================================
      1321  # ENTRY POINT
      1322  # =============================================================================

● Update(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/static/js/toolbar.js)
  ⎿  Added 113 lines
      1536              console.error('Upload failed:', err);
      1537          }
      1538      }
      1539 +
      1540 +    // =========================================================================
      1541 +    // BUNDLE SAVE/IMPORT (.portolan)
      1542 +    // =========================================================================
      1543 +
      1544 +    /**
      1545 +     * Save a .portolan bundle for the current document.
      1546 +     *
      1547 +     * Saves current markups first (same pattern as _handleExport), then calls
      1548 +     * GET /api/documents/{id}/export-bundle and triggers a browser download.
      1549 +     * The bundle preserves the original PDF + all markups, layers, and scale
      1550 +     * settings so the document can be fully re-edited on any PortolanCAST
      1551 +     * installation.
      1552 +     */
      1553 +    async _handleBundleSave() {
      1554 +        if (!this.viewer || !this.viewer.docId) {
      1555 +            alert('No document open to save as bundle.');
      1556 +            return;
      1557 +        }
      1558 +
      1559 +        const statusMsg = document.getElementById('status-message');
      1560 +        statusMsg.textContent = 'Saving bundle...';
      1561 +
      1562 +        try {
      1563 +            // Save current page state to in-memory map before bundling
      1564 +            if (this.canvas && this.canvas.onContentChange) {
      1565 +                this.canvas.onPageChanging(this.viewer.currentPage);
      1566 +            }
      1567 +
      1568 +            // Flush all markups to the server so the bundle has current data
      1569 +            const pages = this.canvas ? this.canvas.getAllPageMarkups() : {};
      1570 +            const saveResp = await fetch(
      1571 +                `/api/documents/${this.viewer.docId}/markups`,
      1572 +                {
      1573 +                    method: 'PUT',
      1574 +                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      1575 +                    body: JSON.stringify({ pages }),
      1576 +                }
      1577 +            );
      1578 +            if (!saveResp.ok) {
      1579 +                throw new Error('Failed to save markups before bundling');
      1580 +            }
      1581 +
      1582 +            // Fetch the bundle as a binary blob
      1583 +            const bundleResp = await fetch(
      1584 +                `/api/documents/${this.viewer.docId}/export-bundle`
      1585 +            );
      1586 +            if (!bundleResp.ok) {
      1587 +                const err = await bundleResp.json();
      1588 +                throw new Error(err.detail || 'Bundle export failed');
      1589 +            }
      1590 +
      1591 +            const blob = await bundleResp.blob();
      1592 +
      1593 +            // Trigger browser download using the server-provided filename
      1594 +            const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
      1595 +            const a = document.createElement('a');
      1596 +            a.href = url;
      1597 +            const cd = bundleResp.headers.get('Content-Disposition');
      1598 +            const match = cd && cd.match(/filename="?([^"]+)"?/);
      1599 +            a.download = match ? match[1] : 'document.portolan';
      1600 +            document.body.appendChild(a);
      1601 +            a.click();
      1602 +            document.body.removeChild(a);
      1603 +            URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
      1604 +
      1605 +            statusMsg.textContent = 'Bundle saved';
      1606 +        } catch (err) {
      1607 +            statusMsg.textContent = `Bundle save error: ${err.message}`;
      1608 +            console.error('[Toolbar] Bundle save failed:', err);
      1609 +        }
      1610 +    }
      1611 +
      1612 +    /**
      1613 +     * Import a .portolan bundle and open the restored document.
      1614 +     *
      1615 +     * POSTs the bundle file to /api/documents/import-bundle, which creates
      1616 +     * a new document record and restores all markup/layer/scale state.
      1617 +     * On success, navigates to the new document's editor page.
      1618 +     *
      1619 +     * Args:
      1620 +     *   file: File object from the file input (must end with .portolan).
      1621 +     */
      1622 +    async _handleBundleImport(file) {
      1623 +        const statusMsg = document.getElementById('status-message');
      1624 +        statusMsg.textContent = `Importing ${file.name}...`;
      1625 +
      1626 +        const formData = new FormData();
      1627 +        formData.append('file', file);
      1628 +
      1629 +        try {
      1630 +            const resp = await fetch('/api/documents/import-bundle', {
      1631 +                method: 'POST',
      1632 +                body: formData,
      1633 +            });
      1634 +
      1635 +            if (!resp.ok) {
      1636 +                const err = await resp.json();
      1637 +                throw new Error(err.detail || 'Import failed');
      1638 +            }
      1639 +
      1640 +            const result = await resp.json();
      1641 +            statusMsg.textContent = `Imported: ${result.filename}`;
      1642 +
      1643 +            // Navigate to the newly created document
      1644 +            window.history.pushState({}, '', `/edit/${result.id}`);
      1645 +            await this.viewer.loadDocument(result.id);
      1646 +
      1647 +        } catch (err) {
      1648 +            statusMsg.textContent = `Import error: ${err.message}`;
      1649 +            console.error('[Toolbar] Bundle import failed:', err);
      1650 +        }
      1651 +    }
      1652  }
