● Both endpoints work. Now write the test file:

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The test structure mirrors the plan exactly: UI Shell tests verify static HTML presence (fast, no JS eval needed),
  then endpoint tests hit the API directly from Node.js fetch, and the round-trip test verifies semantic equivalence —
  the imported page_count must match the exported page_count. This pyramid (HTML → API → round-trip) catches failures at
   progressively higher integration levels.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_bundle.mjs)
  ⎿  Wrote 197 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/OpenRevu/test_bundle.mjs
        1 /**
        2  * PortolanCAST — Bundle Export/Import Browser Tests
        3  *
        4  * Tests the .portolan ZIP bundle format:
        5  *   - UI presence of Save Bundle button and updated file-input accept
        6  *   - GET /api/documents/{id}/export-bundle endpoint (HTTP, content-type, filename, contents)
        7  *   - POST /api/documents/import-bundle round-trip (import → doc accessible)
        8  *
        9  * Run via Windows Chrome from WSL:
       10  *   cmd.exe /c "cd C:\Users\User1\ClaudeProjects\OpenRevu && node test_bundle.mjs"
       11  *
       12  * Author: PortolanCAST
       13  * Version: 1.0.0
       14  * Date: 2026-02-24
       15  */
       16
       17 import { chromium } from 'playwright';
       18 import { createWriteStream } from 'fs';
       19 import { unlink, readFile, writeFile } from 'fs/promises';
       20 import path from 'path';
       21 import os from 'os';
       22
       23 const BASE_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';
       24 const DOC_ID = 1;
       25 let passed = 0;
       26 let failed = 0;
       27
       28 function assert(condition, msg) {
       29     if (condition) {
       30         passed++;
       31         console.log(`  PASS: ${msg}`);
       32     } else {
       33         failed++;
       34         console.log(`  FAIL: ${msg}`);
       35     }
       36 }
       37
       38 async function run() {
       39     const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
       40     const page = await browser.newPage();
       41
       42     // Temp file paths used by export+import round-trip
       43     const bundlePath = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `test_portolan_${Date.now()}.portolan`);
       44     let importedDocId = null;
       45
       46     try {
       47         // =====================================================================
       48         // Group 1: UI Shell (2 tests)
       49         // =====================================================================
       50         console.log('\n  -- Group 1: UI Shell --');
       51
       52         await page.goto(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${DOC_ID}`, { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
       53         await page.waitForTimeout(1200);
       54
       55         // Test 1: #btn-save-bundle exists inside .toolbar-export
       56         const bundleBtnInExport = await page.evaluate(() => {
       57             const btn = document.getElementById('btn-save-bundle');
       58             if (!btn) return false;
       59             const parent = btn.closest('.toolbar-export');
       60             return parent !== null;
       61         });
       62         assert(bundleBtnInExport, '#btn-save-bundle exists inside .toolbar-export');
       63
       64         // Test 2: #file-input accept attribute includes .portolan
       65         const fileInputAccept = await page.evaluate(() => {
       66             const input = document.getElementById('file-input');
       67             return input ? input.getAttribute('accept') : '';
       68         });
       69         assert(
       70             fileInputAccept && fileInputAccept.includes('.portolan'),
       71             `#file-input accept contains .portolan (got "${fileInputAccept}")`
       72         );
       73
       74         // =====================================================================
       75         // Group 2: Export Bundle Endpoint (5 tests)
       76         // =====================================================================
       77         console.log('\n  -- Group 2: Export Bundle Endpoint --');
       78
       79         // Test 3: GET /api/documents/1/export-bundle → 200
       80         const exportResp = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/documents/${DOC_ID}/export-bundle`);
       81         assert(exportResp.status === 200, `GET /api/documents/${DOC_ID}/export-bundle → 200`);
       82
       83         // Test 4: Content-Type is application/zip
       84         const contentType = exportResp.headers.get('content-type') || '';
       85         assert(
       86             contentType.includes('application/zip'),
       87             `Content-Type is application/zip (got "${contentType}")`
       88         );
       89
       90         // Test 5: Content-Disposition filename contains .portolan
       91         const cd = exportResp.headers.get('content-disposition') || '';
       92         assert(
       93             cd.includes('.portolan'),
       94             `Content-Disposition contains .portolan (got "${cd}")`
       95         );
       96
       97         // Download the bundle to disk for inspection and re-import
       98         const bundleBytes = await exportResp.arrayBuffer();
       99         await writeFile(bundlePath, Buffer.from(bundleBytes));
      100
      101         // Test 6: ZIP namelist contains all 5 required files
      102         // Use Python to inspect the ZIP (Node stdlib has no built-in ZIP reader)
      103         const { execSync } = await import('child_process');
      104         const zipContents = execSync(
      105             `python3 -c "import zipfile,json; zf=zipfile.ZipFile('${bundlePath}'); print(json.dumps(sor
          ted(zf.namelist())))"`
      106         ).toString().trim();
      107         const zipFiles = JSON.parse(zipContents);
      108         const requiredFiles = ['metadata.json', 'original.pdf', 'markups.json', 'layers.json', 'scale.j
          son'];
      109         const allPresent = requiredFiles.every(f => zipFiles.includes(f));
      110         assert(allPresent, `ZIP contains all 5 required files (got ${zipFiles.join(', ')})`);
      111
      112         // Test 7: metadata.json has version, filename, page_count fields
      113         const metaJson = execSync(
      114             `python3 -c "import zipfile,json; zf=zipfile.ZipFile('${bundlePath}'); print(zf.read('metad
          ata.json').decode())"`
      115         ).toString().trim();
      116         const meta = JSON.parse(metaJson);
      117         assert(
      118             meta.version && meta.filename && meta.page_count,
      119             `metadata.json has version="${meta.version}", filename="${meta.filename}", page_count=${met
          a.page_count}`
      120         );
      121
      122         // =====================================================================
      123         // Group 3: Import Round-trip (3 tests)
      124         // =====================================================================
      125         console.log('\n  -- Group 3: Import Round-trip --');
      126
      127         // Test 8: POST exported bundle to /api/documents/import-bundle → 200 with id
      128         // Use FormData + fetch from Node (Node 18+ supports FormData natively)
      129         const { readFile: readFileCb } = await import('fs/promises');
      130         const bundleBuffer = await readFileCb(bundlePath);
      131         const formData = new FormData();
      132         const blob = new Blob([bundleBuffer], { type: 'application/zip' });
      133         formData.append('file', blob, 'test.portolan');
      134
      135         const importResp = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/documents/import-bundle`, {
      136             method: 'POST',
      137             body: formData,
      138         });
      139         assert(importResp.status === 200, `POST /api/documents/import-bundle → 200`);
      140
      141         let importResult = null;
      142         if (importResp.ok) {
      143             importResult = await importResp.json();
      144             importedDocId = importResult.id;
      145         }
      146         assert(
      147             importResult && typeof importResult.id === 'number',
      148             `Import returns { id: ${importResult?.id} }`
      149         );
      150
      151         // Test 9: GET /edit/{id} → 200 (imported doc accessible)
      152         if (importedDocId) {
      153             const editResp = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/edit/${importedDocId}`);
      154             assert(editResp.status === 200, `GET /edit/${importedDocId} → 200 (imported doc accessible)
          `);
      155         } else {
      156             failed++;
      157             console.log('  FAIL: /edit/{id} check skipped — import did not return id');
      158         }
      159
      160         // Test 10: Imported doc page_count matches original
      161         if (importedDocId) {
      162             const infoResp = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/documents/${importedDocId}/info`);
      163             const info = await infoResp.json();
      164             assert(
      165                 info.page_count === meta.page_count,
      166                 `Imported page_count (${info.page_count}) matches original (${meta.page_count})`
      167             );
      168         } else {
      169             failed++;
      170             console.log('  FAIL: page_count check skipped — import did not return id');
      171         }
      172
      173     } finally {
      174         await browser.close();
      175
      176         // Cleanup temp bundle file
      177         try { await unlink(bundlePath); } catch (_) {}
      178
      179         // Delete the imported test document to keep DB clean
      180         if (importedDocId) {
      181             try {
      182                 await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/documents/${importedDocId}`, { method: 'DELETE' });
      183             } catch (_) {}
      184         }
      185
      186         console.log('\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════');
      187         console.log(`  Results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed, ${passed + failed} total`);
      188         console.log('══════════════════════════════════════════════════');
      189
      190         if (failed > 0) process.exit(1);
      191     }
      192 }
      193
      194 run().catch(err => {
      195     console.error('Fatal test error:', err);
      196     process.exit(1);
      197 });

  No ZIP library in node_modules. I need to implement ZIP inspection in pure Node.js. ZIP files have a specific format I
   can parse manually, or I can take a different approach for tests 6 and 7.

  The approach: Instead of using Python or a ZIP library, I can validate the ZIP structure by:
  1. Verifying the file starts with the ZIP magic bytes PK (0x50 0x4B)
  2. Posting the bundle to the import endpoint (which validates structure server-side) and checking the response - this
  implicitly validates the ZIP contains metadata.json and original.pdf
  3. Then fetching the original doc's info and the imported doc's info to validate page_count matches

  4. For test 6, I'll just check the magic bytes and confirm the import succeeds, which proves the ZIP structure is
  valid since the server validates it.
  5. For test 7, I'll verify the metadata fields directly from the import response.

  Actually, I could implement a minimal ZIP central directory reader in JavaScript to parse the structure myself, but
  that's getting complex. Let me reconsider using WSL Python instead—I can call wsl python3 directly from Node's
  execSync on Windows, which might be simpler than building a ZIP parser. Or even better, Node has zlib built-in, so I
  could decompress the ZIP without external dependencies.

  Actually, the cleanest approach is to validate the bundle through the server's import endpoint first, then cross-check
   the response fields against the metadata. For the ZIP contents check, I'll try calling WSL Python from Node since
  it's available on the system.

  Headers are parseable) to avoid the Windows python3 dependency:

      14   *

      37      }
      38  }
      39
      40 +/**
      41 + * Parse filenames from a ZIP buffer using Local File Header signatures.
      42 + *
      43 + * ZIP Local File Header format (per PKZIP spec):
      44 + *   Offset  Length  Field
      45 + *   0       4       Signature: 0x04034b50 (little-endian PK\x03\x04)
      46 + *   ...
      47 + *   26      2       Filename length (n)
      48 + *   28      2       Extra field length (m)
      49 + *   30      n       Filename
      50 + *   30+n    m       Extra field
      51 + *   30+n+m  ...     File data
      52 + *
      53 + * We walk the buffer looking for each PK\x03\x04 signature to collect names.
      54 + *
      55 + * Args:
      56 + *   buf: Node.js Buffer of the full ZIP file.
      57 + *
      58 + * Returns:
      59 + *   Array of filename strings found in the local file headers.
      60 + */
      61 +function zipFilenames(buf) {
      62 +    const names = [];
      63 +    let i = 0;
      64 +    const SIG = 0x04034b50;  // local file header signature (little-endian stored as LE UInt32)
      65 +    while (i < buf.length - 30) {
      66 +        if (buf.readUInt32LE(i) === SIG) {
      67 +            const nameLen  = buf.readUInt16LE(i + 26);
      68 +            const extraLen = buf.readUInt16LE(i + 28);
      69 +            const name = buf.slice(i + 30, i + 30 + nameLen).toString('utf8');
      70 +            names.push(name);
      71 +            // Advance past this entry's header + extra + compressed data
      72 +            // We don't decompress — just skip using the compressed size field (offset 18)
      73 +            const compressedSize = buf.readUInt32LE(i + 18);
      74 +            i += 30 + nameLen + extraLen + compressedSize;
      75 +        } else {
      76 +            i++;
      77 +        }
      78 +    }
      79 +    return names;
      80 +}
      81 +

      83      const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
      84      const page = await browser.newPage();

● Write(/mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/PKM/Inbox/MechDraw_Plugin_Spec_2_24_2026.md)
  ⎿  Wrote 330 lines to /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/PKM/Inbox/MechDraw_Plugin_Spec_2_24_2026.md
        1 # MechDraw AI Plugin — Design Specification
        2
        3 **Status:** Phase X — Future (not yet scheduled)
        4 **Author:** PortolanCAST
        5 **Date:** 2026-02-24
        6 **Related:** PortolanCAST Plugin Loader API (Phase 4B), `plugins.js`, `static/js/plugins/`
        7
        8 ---
        9
       10 ## Summary
       11
       12 MechDraw is a proposed PortolanCAST plugin that converts natural-language duct layout
       13 descriptions into Fabric.js objects on the canvas. The user types a prompt like
       14 "run a 24×12 duct from the AHU northeast 40 feet, then branch left 20 feet" and
       15 MechDraw generates the corresponding Path/Group objects with HVAC markupType metadata.
       16
       17 This spec covers the architecture, integration points, deferred decisions, and security
       18 constraints. It does not commit to a timeline. Implementation requires:
       19
       20 1. A working Ollama instance with a vision-capable model (e.g. llava:7b or mistrall3:8b)
       21 2. A structured output schema stable enough to parse reliably
       22 3. At least one completed review of the PortolanCAST coordinate system
       23
       24 ---
       25
       26 ## Problem Statement
       27
       28 HVAC and mechanical engineers frequently sketch duct layouts on existing architectural
       29 drawings. Current PortolanCAST tools require hand-drawing each duct segment individually.
       30 MechDraw would let a user describe a duct run in plain language, optionally anchored to
       31 visible features in the drawing, and have the tool generate parametric Fabric.js objects.
       32
       33 **Target user story:**
       34 > "I want to say 'run a 24×12 rectangular duct from grid intersection A-3 northeast
       35 > 30 feet, then turn east 15 feet to the diffuser at A-5' and have it appear on the canvas
       36 > without me measuring and clicking."
       37
       38 ---
       39
       40 ## Input Modes
       41
       42 ### Mode 1: Text Prompt (required for v1)
       43
       44 The user types a natural-language description in the plugin panel's text input.
       45 MechDraw parses the prompt using an Ollama LLM with a structured output prompt
       46 that extracts:
       47
       48 ```json
       49 {
       50   "segments": [
       51     {
       52       "type": "duct_rect",
       53       "width_in": 24,
       54       "height_in": 12,
       55       "start": {"x": 0, "y": 0},
       56       "end": {"x": 500, "y": 0},
       57       "label": "Supply Duct — 24×12"
       58     }
       59   ],
       60   "anchors": []
       61 }
       62 ```
       63
       64 Coordinates are in **canvas pixel space** (BASE_DPI=150, same as all other tools).
       65 The plugin must convert real-world dimensions to pixels using the document's
       66 `ScaleManager` (via `app.scale`).
       67
       68 ### Mode 2: Prompt + Floor Plan Image (deferred — v2+)
       69
       70 The user provides both a text prompt AND an image crop from the current PDF view.
       71 Ollama's vision endpoint (`/api/generate` with `images: [base64]`) receives the crop
       72 so the model can spatially anchor the duct run to visible features (grid lines, walls,
       73 equipment labels).
       74
       75 **Deferred reason:** Vision model output is less deterministic. Requires a calibration
       76 strategy and more robust error handling before production use.
       77
       78 ---
       79
       80 ## Output: Fabric.js Objects
       81
       82 MechDraw injects PortolanCAST-compatible Fabric.js objects directly onto the active
       83 canvas page. Each duct segment becomes one or more Fabric objects:
       84
       85 ### Rectangular Duct → `fabric.Rect`
       86
       87 ```javascript
       88 new fabric.Rect({
       89     left: segment.start.x,
       90     top:  segment.start.y,
       91     width:  pixelLength,
       92     height: pixelWidth,
       93     fill: 'transparent',
       94     stroke: '#1565C0',
       95     strokeWidth: 2,
       96     angle: angleDegrees,
       97     // PortolanCAST required metadata
       98     markupType:   'change',    // HVAC additions = change intent by default
       99     markupStatus: 'open',
      100     markupNote:   segment.label,
      101     markupAuthor: 'MechDraw',
      102     markupTimestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      103 })
      104 ```
      105
      106 ### Duct Run with Label → `fabric.Group`
      107
      108 Complex runs (bends, branches) are grouped with a companion IText label showing
      109 duct size (e.g., "24×12"). The group's `markupType` follows PortolanCAST conventions.
      110
      111 ### Object Injection
      112
      113 Objects are injected using `canvas.stampDefaults(obj)` (applied after construction,
      114 per PortolanCAST convention) followed by `canvas.fabricCanvas.add(obj)`. The plugin
      115 must call `canvas.onContentChange()` after injection to trigger auto-save.
      116
      117 ---
      118
      119 ## Integration: PluginLoader API
      120
      121 MechDraw registers via the existing PluginLoader (Phase 4B):
      122
      123 ```javascript
      124 // In static/js/plugins/mechdraw.js
      125 const MechDraw = {
      126     name: 'mechdraw',
      127     tabLabel: 'MechDraw',
      128
      129     onReady(app) {
      130         this._app = app;
      131         this._renderPanel();
      132     },
      133
      134     onDocumentLoaded(docId) {
      135         this._currentDocId = docId;
      136     },
      137
      138     // Called by toolbar.js when user presses Generate
      139     async generate(prompt) {
      140         const scale = this._app.scale;
      141         const resp  = await fetch('/api/mechdraw/generate', {
      142             method: 'POST',
      143             headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      144             body: JSON.stringify({ prompt, doc_id: this._currentDocId }),
      145         });
      146         const { segments } = await resp.json();
      147         for (const seg of segments) {
      148             this._placeSegment(seg, scale);
      149         }
      150         this._app.canvas.onContentChange();
      151     },
      152
      153     _placeSegment(seg, scale) { /* ... */ },
      154     _renderPanel() { /* inject panel HTML */ },
      155 };
      156
      157 // Register on load
      158 if (window.plugins) {
      159     window.plugins.register(MechDraw);
      160 }
      161 ```
      162
      163 **Lifecycle hooks used:**
      164 - `onReady` — panel render, store `app` reference
      165 - `onDocumentLoaded` — reset prompt, update doc_id
      166
      167 **Lifecycle hooks NOT used (yet):**
      168 - `onPageChanged` — not needed; segments target the active page
      169 - `onMarkupSelected` — not applicable
      170
      171 ---
      172
      173 ## Backend: `/api/mechdraw/generate`
      174
      175 New FastAPI endpoint in `main.py`:
      176
      177 ```python
      178 @app.post("/api/mechdraw/generate")
      179 async def mechdraw_generate(doc_id: int, request: Request):
      180     """
      181     Convert a natural-language duct layout prompt to Fabric.js segment descriptors.
      182
      183     Calls Ollama with a structured output prompt. Returns an array of segment
      184     objects that the frontend converts to Fabric canvas objects.
      185
      186     Security:
      187         - doc_id validated against DB (document must exist)
      188         - prompt is passed to Ollama only (never eval'd or executed)
      189         - Response is JSON-parsed; malformed output returns empty segments
      190     """
      191     body = await request.json()
      192     prompt = str(body.get("prompt", ""))[:1000]   # SECURITY: cap length
      193     ...
      194 ```
      195
      196 **Ollama call pattern:**
      197
      198 ```python
      199 system_prompt = """
      200 You are a mechanical engineering assistant. Convert duct layout descriptions
      201 to JSON segment arrays. Output ONLY valid JSON — no prose, no markdown.
      202
      203 Schema for each segment:
      204 {
      205   "type": "duct_rect",       // always "duct_rect" for now
      206   "width_in": <number>,      // duct width in inches
      207   "height_in": <number>,     // duct height in inches
      208   "start_real": {"x": <ft>, "y": <ft>},   // start in real-world feet
      209   "end_real":   {"x": <ft>, "y": <ft>},   // end in real-world feet
      210   "label": "<string>"
      211 }
      212 """
      213 ```
      214
      215 Coordinate conversion (real feet → canvas pixels):
      216
      217 ```python
      218 def feet_to_pixels(feet: float, paper_inches_per_unit: float, render_dpi: float = 150.0) -> float:
      219     # paper_inches_per_unit: from document scale setting
      220     # RENDER_DPI: the rendering constant (always 150 in PortolanCAST)
      221     return feet * render_dpi * paper_inches_per_unit
      222 ```
      223
      224 ---
      225
      226 ## Coordinate Strategy
      227
      228 All generated objects must respect the PortolanCAST coordinate system:
      229
      230 | Concept | Value |
      231 |---------|-------|
      232 | Render DPI | 150 px/inch |
      233 | Scale setting | `paper_inches_per_unit` from DB |
      234 | Canvas origin | top-left (0,0) |
      235 | Zoom | applied via `Fabric.setZoom(scale)` — objects are in natural coords |
      236
      237 The plugin should read scale from `app.scale.getScale()` (returns `{pixels_per_unit}`),
      238 then multiply real-world distances by `pixels_per_unit` to get canvas pixel values.
      239
      240 **Example:** At 1/4" = 1ft scale, `paper_inches_per_unit = 0.25`.
      241 `pixels_per_unit = 150 × 0.25 = 37.5 px/ft`
      242 A 40ft duct → `40 × 37.5 = 1500 px`
      243
      244 ---
      245
      246 ## Security Constraints
      247
      248 Per PortolanCAST's cybernetic security framework:
      249
      250 1. **Prompt length limit:** cap at 1000 chars server-side (prevent oversized Ollama calls)
      251 2. **Output validation:** JSON-parse the LLM response; catch exceptions; return `{segments: []}`
      252    on any parse failure rather than propagating the error
      253 3. **Coordinate bounds:** clamp generated coordinates to `[0, page_width_px × 2]` —
      254    discard segments outside reasonable canvas bounds
      255 4. **No code execution:** LLM output is never eval'd. Only the JSON segment schema
      256    is consumed; all other fields are ignored.
      257 5. **SECURITY comment on Ollama call:**
      258    ```python
      259    # SECURITY: prompt goes to local Ollama only (127.0.0.1:11434)
      260    # Never send user prompts to external APIs without explicit consent
      261    ```
      262
      263 ---
      264
      265 ## Panel UI (Conceptual)
      266
      267 ```
      268 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
      269 │  MechDraw AI                                │
      270 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
      271 │  Describe the duct layout:                  │
      272 │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐   │
      273 │  │ 24x12 supply duct from AHU north 40 │   │
      274 │  │ ft, branch east 20 ft to grille     │   │
      275 │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘   │
      276 │                                             │
      277 │  Start position:  [ x: 0  y: 0  ] (canvas) │
      278 │                                             │
      279 │  [ Generate ]  [ Clear ]                   │
      280 │                                             │
      281 │  ⚠ Uses local Ollama (llava:7b or similar) │
      282 └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
      283 ```
      284
      285 ---
      286
      287 ## Open Questions (Deferred)
      288
      289 1. **Model selection:** Which Ollama model produces the most reliable structured JSON?
      290    Candidates: `mistral:7b`, `llama3:8b`, `llava:7b` (vision). Needs benchmarking.
      291
      292 2. **Anchor detection:** How does the user specify "start at grid A-3"? Options:
      293    a. Click-to-anchor before typing (user clicks canvas, plugin captures coordinates)
      294    b. OCR grid labels from the PDF page image and resolve semantically
      295    c. Require explicit pixel coordinates in the prompt (least magical)
      296
      297 3. **Undo integration:** Should MechDraw-generated objects participate in
      298    PortolanCAST's canvas undo/redo stack? They are Fabric objects so they can,
      299    but the history entry should group all segments from one generation as one undo step.
      300
      301 4. **Multi-floor plans:** When the document has multiple pages, MechDraw should
      302    scope generation to the current page only. Cross-page duct runs are out of scope.
      303
      304 5. **Fitting/equipment symbols:** How to represent AHUs, diffusers, VAV boxes?
      305    Could use SVG Paths stored as templates, or plain Rect with IText labels.
      306
      307 ---
      308
      309 ## Integration Checklist (Before Implementation)
      310
      311 - [ ] Confirm Ollama model produces stable JSON output for 10+ test prompts
      312 - [ ] Add `/api/mechdraw/generate` route to PIPELINE.md as Phase X
      313 - [ ] Design start-anchor UI (click-to-set origin point)
      314 - [ ] Write unit tests for `feet_to_pixels()` conversion
      315 - [ ] Write browser tests: prompt → segments → canvas objects count
      316 - [ ] Add `mechdraw` to `plugins.js` HOOK_MAP if new hooks needed
      317 - [ ] Performance test: measure Ollama round-trip latency at 7B/13B model sizes
      318
      319 ---
      320
      321 ## References
      322
      323 - PortolanCAST Plugin Loader API: `static/js/plugins.js`
      324 - PortolanCAST EC Plugin (reference implementation): `static/js/plugins/extended-cognition.js`
      325 - PortolanCAST ScaleManager: `static/js/scale.js`
      326 - Canvas coordinate strategy: `static/js/canvas.js` (BASE_DPI constant)
      327 - Ollama API docs: `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate` (local)
      328 - HVAC duct sizing reference: SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards
      329 - Portolan charts (name origin): Medieval maritime navigation charts (1200s–1600s),
      330   the first technically precise maps — an apt metaphor for PortolanCAST's purpose.

│ Plan to implement                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ PortolanCAST — Rename + Bundle Export                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │

│ Goals:                                                                                                               │
│ 1. Rename the project from "OpenRevu" to "PortolanCAST" across all source files                                      │
│ 2. Add a portable save format (.portolan ZIP bundle) for full re-editing across machines                             │

│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Context                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ The project has outgrown its working name. "OpenRevu" implied a clone of Bluebeam                                    │
│ Revu; "PortolanCAST" is original — portolan charts were the first precise technical                                  │
│ navigation maps (1200s–1600s), a fitting metaphor for a tool that annotates and                                      │
│ measures technical drawings. CAST = Computer-Aided Systems Thinking.                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Bundle export solves the "share-for-review" workflow: export a .portolan ZIP that                                    │
│ contains the original PDF plus all markups, layers, and scale settings. The recipient                                │
│ opens it like any PDF — the full session is restored exactly.                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │

│ - "openrevu" → "portolancast" (lowercase, used in DB path and status messages)                                       │
│ - "OpenRevu Team" → "PortolanCAST" (author field in file headers)                                                    │

│                                                                                                                      │
│ db.py line 30: DEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "data" / "openrevu.db"                                      │

│ # Example (run per-file or with find):                                                                               │
│ sed -i 's/OpenRevu/PortolanCAST/g; s/openrevu/portolancast/g' main.py db.py ...                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Phase 1 — Bundle Export/Import (.portolan)                                                                           │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Bundle Format                                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ .portolan is a ZIP (DEFLATED) containing:                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ┌───────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                                 │
│ │     File      │                             Contents                             │                                 │
│ ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                                 │
│ │ metadata.json │ {version, app:"PortolanCAST", filename, page_count, exported_at} │                                 │
│ ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                                 │
│ │ original.pdf  │ Byte-exact copy of the source PDF                                │                                 │
│ ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                                 │
│ │ markups.json  │ {pages: {"0": fabricJSON, ...}} — same as markups API            │                                 │
│ ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                                 │
│ │ layers.json   │ {layers:[...], activeId} — same as layers API                    │                                 │
│ ├───────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤                                 │
│ │ scale.json    │ {preset, paper_inches_per_unit, label} — same as scale API       │                                 │
│ └───────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Only metadata.json + original.pdf required; others are optional (import falls                                        │
│ back to defaults). This ensures forward/backward compatibility.                                                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Backend (main.py)                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ New imports (add to existing imports at top):                                                                        │
│ import io                                                                                                            │
│ import zipfile                                                                                                       │
│ from datetime import datetime                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ GET /api/documents/{doc_id}/export-bundle:                                                                           │
│ 1. Verify doc + PDF file exist                                                                                       │
│ 2. Load markups (db.get_markups), layers (db.get_document_setting('layers')),                                        │
│ scale (db.get_document_setting('scale'))                                                                             │
│ 3. Build in-memory zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(), 'w', ZIP_DEFLATED):                                                 │
│   - writestr("metadata.json", ...) with datetime.utcnow().isoformat()                                                │
│   - zf.write(str(pdf_path), "original.pdf") — reads PDF from disk into ZIP                                           │
│   - writestr("markups.json", json.dumps({pages}))                                                                    │
│   - writestr("layers.json", ...) — raw setting or _default_layers()                                                  │
│   - writestr("scale.json", ...) — raw setting or _default_scale()                                                    │
│ 4. return Response(buf.getvalue(), media_type="application/zip",             headers={"Content-Disposition":         │
│ 'attachment; filename="X.portolan"'})                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Download filename: strip .pdf from doc["filename"], append .portolan.                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ POST /api/documents/import-bundle:                                                                                   │
│ Security checks (same pattern as /api/upload):                                                                       │
│   - Extension must be .portolan                                                                                      │
│   - Size <= MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE                                                                                          │
│   - zipfile.BadZipFile caught → 400                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Validation:                                                                                                          │
│   - 'metadata.json' and 'original.pdf' must be in namelist()                                                         │
│   - PDF bytes must start with b'%PDF-'                                                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Restore sequence:                                                                                                    │
│   1. Save PDF to PROJECTS_DIR (uuid filename)                                                                        │
│   2. pdf_engine.get_pdf_info() to validate + get page_count                                                          │
│   3. db.add_document(filename, filepath, page_count, file_size)                                                      │
│   4. If markups.json → db.save_markups(doc_id, pages)                                                                │
│   5. If layers.json  → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'layers', raw)                                                │
│   6. If scale.json   → db.set_document_setting(doc_id, 'scale', raw)                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Return: {id, filename, page_count, redirect: /edit/{id}}                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Frontend (toolbar.js)                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _handleBundleSave() — mirrors _handleExport():                                                                       │
│ 1. Guard !this.viewer.docId → alert                                                                                  │
│ 2. Save markups (same save-first pattern as export)                                                                  │
│ 3. fetch(export-bundle) → blob → temp <a> download (Content-Disposition filename)                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ _handleBundleImport(file) — mirrors _handleFileUpload():                                                             │
│ 1. POST FormData to /api/documents/import-bundle                                                                     │

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The rename phase uses sed -i for bulk substitution — faster than reading+editing each file individually in Claude. The
   key is excluding venv/ and node_modules/ since those are third-party packages where our brand name doesn't belong. We
   also need to handle three case variants: OpenRevu (title), openrevu (lowercase), and OpenRevu Team (author) as
  distinct patterns.

     </head>
     <body>

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- TOP TOOLBAR                                                             -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="toolbar">
         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-brand">
             <span class="brand-name">PortolanCAST</span>
         </div>

         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-file">
             <button id="btn-new" class="toolbar-btn" title="New blank document">
                 <span class="icon">&#128196;</span> New
             </button>
             <button id="btn-open" class="toolbar-btn" title="Open PDF">
                 <span class="icon">&#128194;</span> Open
             </button>
             <span id="filename-display" class="filename-display">No file open</span>
         </div>

         <!-- Mode tabs: Navigate | Markup | Measure
              Tab buttons sit inline in the toolbar bar — no second row.
              Clicking a tab shows its tool panel and hides the others. -->
         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-tabs">
             <button class="toolbar-tab active" data-tab="navigate">Navigate</button>
             <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="markup">Markup</button>
             <button class="toolbar-tab" data-tab="measure">Measure</button>
         </div>

         <!-- Navigate tab: pan and select tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="navigate">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <!-- Hand tool: pan mode — events pass through canvas so viewport scrolls -->
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="hand" title="Hand / Pan (G)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9995;</span> Hand
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="select" title="Select (V)">
                     <span class="icon">&#8630;</span> Select
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Markup tab: annotation drawing tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="markup" style="display:none">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="pen" title="Pen (P)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9999;&#65039;</span> Pen
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="rect" title="Rectangle (R)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9645;</span> Rect
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="ellipse" title="Ellipse (E)">

                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="line" title="Line (L)">

                     <span class="icon">&#9729;</span> Cloud
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="callout" title="Callout (O)">
                     <span class="icon">&#128172;</span> Callout
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Measure tab: takeoff and calibration tools -->
         <div class="tab-panel" data-tab="measure" style="display:none">
             <div class="toolbar-group">
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="distance" title="Distance Ruler (U)">
                     <span class="icon">&#128207;</span> Distance
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="area" title="Area Polygon (A)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9649;</span> Area
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="count" title="Count Markers (N)">
                     <span class="icon">&#35;</span> Count
                 </button>
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="calibrate" title="Calibrate Scale (K)">
                     <span class="icon">&#9878;</span> Calibrate
                 </button>
                 <!-- Node Edit: enter vertex/endpoint edit mode for selected measurement -->
                 <button class="toolbar-btn tool-btn" data-tool="node-edit" title="Node Edit — vertex/endpoint
     handles for selected measurement">
                     <span class="icon">&#11835;</span> Node Edit
                 </button>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Settings gear — always visible, not inside any tab panel -->
         <button id="btn-toolbar-settings" class="toolbar-btn toolbar-settings-btn" title="Toolbar settings —
     show/hide tools">&#9881;</button>

         <!-- Intent mode indicator — shows active markup type -->
         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-intent">
             <span id="intent-indicator" class="intent-indicator" title="Active markup type (1-5 to change)">
                 <span id="intent-dot" class="intent-dot"></span>
                 <span id="intent-label">Note</span>
             </span>
         </div>

         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-export">
             <button id="btn-export" class="toolbar-btn"
                     title="Export annotated PDF — markups become permanent (not editable after export)">
                 <span class="icon">&#128190;</span> Export PDF
             </button>
         </div>

         <div class="toolbar-group toolbar-zoom">
             <button id="btn-zoom-out" class="toolbar-btn" title="Zoom Out (-)">−</button>
             <span id="zoom-display" class="zoom-display">100%</span>
             <button id="btn-zoom-in" class="toolbar-btn" title="Zoom In (+)">+</button>
             <button id="btn-zoom-fit" class="toolbar-btn" title="Fit to Width">Fit</button>
         </div>
     </div>

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- MAIN CONTENT AREA                                                       -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="main-content">

         <!-- LEFT PANEL: Pages + Markups (tabbed) -->
         <div id="panel-left" class="side-panel">
             <div class="panel-tabs">
                 <button class="panel-tab active" data-panel="pages">Pages</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="markups">Markups</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="measures">Measures</button>
                 <button class="panel-tab" data-panel="layers">Layers</button>
             </div>

             <!-- Pages tab content -->
             <div id="tab-pages" class="tab-content active">
                 <div id="thumbnail-list" class="thumbnail-list">
                     <!-- Thumbnails inserted dynamically -->
                 </div>
                 <!-- Add blank page — only shown when a document is open -->
                 <div id="pages-panel-footer" class="pages-panel-footer" style="display: none;">
                     <button id="btn-add-page" class="btn-add-page" title="Append a blank page to this document">
                         + Add Page
                     </button>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Markups tab content -->
             <div id="tab-markups" class="tab-content">
                 <!-- Summary bar: count + filter -->
                 <div id="markup-list-header">
                     <span id="markup-count">0 markups</span>
                     <select id="markup-filter" title="Filter by type">
                         <option value="all">All Types</option>
                         <optgroup label="Markups">
                             <option value="note">Note</option>
                             <option value="issue">Issue</option>
                             <option value="question">Question</option>
                             <option value="approval">Approval</option>
                             <option value="change">Change</option>
                         </optgroup>
                         <optgroup label="Measurements">
                             <option value="distance">Distance</option>
                             <option value="area">Area</option>
                             <option value="count">Count</option>
                         </optgroup>
                     </select>
                     <select id="markup-status-filter" title="Filter by status">
                         <option value="all">All Status</option>
                         <option value="open">Open</option>
                         <option value="resolved">Resolved</option>
                     </select>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Scrollable markup rows -->
                 <div id="markup-list" class="markup-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="markup-list-empty">No markups yet</p>
                     <!-- Rows inserted dynamically by markup-list.js -->
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Layers tab content — Phase 5 layer management -->
             <div id="tab-layers" class="tab-content">
                 <div id="layers-header">
                     <span id="layers-count">0 layers</span>
                     <button id="btn-new-layer" class="toolbar-btn layers-new-btn"
                             title="Add a new layer">+ Layer</button>
                 </div>
                 <div id="layers-list" class="layers-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="layers-empty">No layers</p>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Measures tab content — Phase 4A measurement summary panel -->
             <div id="tab-measures" class="tab-content">
                 <!-- Stat cards: one per measurement type -->
                 <div id="measure-stats" class="measure-stats">
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-distance-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Distances</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-distance-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-area-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Areas</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-area-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="measure-stat-card">
                         <div class="stat-count" id="stat-count-count">0</div>
                         <div class="stat-label">Count Items</div>
                         <div class="stat-total" id="stat-count-total">—</div>
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Filter row + export button -->
                 <div id="measure-list-header">
                     <select id="measure-type-filter">
                         <option value="all">All Types</option>
                         <option value="distance">Distance</option>
                         <option value="area">Area</option>
                         <option value="count">Count</option>
                     </select>
                     <button id="btn-export-csv" class="toolbar-btn measure-export-btn"
                             title="Export measurements to CSV">Export CSV</button>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Scrollable measurement rows — inserted dynamically by measure-summary.js -->
                 <div id="measure-list" class="markup-list">
                     <p class="muted-text" id="measure-list-empty">No measurements yet</p>
                 </div>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- CENTER: PDF Canvas viewport -->
         <div id="viewport">
             <!-- Welcome screen (shown when no document is open) -->
             <div id="welcome-screen">
                 <div class="welcome-content">
                     <h1>PortolanCAST</h1>
                     <p>Open-source PDF markup &amp; measurement</p>
                     <button id="btn-welcome-open" class="welcome-btn">Open a PDF</button>


                 </div>
             </div>

             <!-- Canvas container (hidden until a PDF is loaded) -->
             <div id="canvas-container" style="display: none;">
                 <!-- PDF rendered image -->
                 <img id="pdf-image" alt="PDF page" draggable="false">
                 <!-- Fabric.js markup overlay — positioned absolutely over pdf-image via CSS -->
                 <canvas id="fabric-canvas"></canvas>
             </div>
         </div>

         <!-- RIGHT PANEL: Properties / Plugin space -->
         <div id="panel-properties" class="side-panel">
             <!-- Tab bar — Properties always present; plugin tabs injected by PluginLoader.register() -->
             <div class="panel-tabs" id="right-panel-tabs">
                 <button class="panel-tab active" data-panel="properties">Properties</button>
             </div>

             <!-- Properties tab — wraps existing content unchanged -->
             <div id="tab-properties" class="tab-content active">
             <div id="properties-content" class="panel-body">

                 <!-- Measurement properties — shown when a measurement object is selected -->
                 <!-- Hidden by default; shown instead of #markup-semantic-props for measurements -->
                 <div id="measurement-props" style="display: none;">
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Measurement</div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label id="measure-label">Value:</label>
                         <span id="measure-value" class="measure-value-large">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <!-- Secondary row: total count (count tool) or pixel value (all tools) -->
                     <div class="prop-row" id="measure-secondary-row" style="display: none;">
                         <label id="measure-secondary-label">Total:</label>
                         <span id="measure-secondary-value">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Scale:</label>
                         <span id="measure-scale-label" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>

                 <!-- Markup properties — shown when a markup object is selected -->
                 <div id="markup-props" style="display: none;">
                     <!-- Semantic fields — hidden for measurement objects -->
                     <div id="markup-semantic-props">
                         <div class="prop-section-header">Markup</div>
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Object:</label>
                             <span id="prop-obj-type">—</span>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-type">Type</label>
                             <select id="prop-markup-type">
                                 <option value="note">Note</option>
                                 <option value="issue">Issue</option>
                                 <option value="question">Question</option>
                                 <option value="approval">Approval</option>
                                 <option value="change">Change</option>
                             </select>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-status">Status</label>
                             <select id="prop-markup-status">
                                 <option value="open">Open</option>
                                 <option value="resolved">Resolved</option>
                             </select>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-field">
                             <label for="prop-markup-note">Note</label>
                             <textarea id="prop-markup-note" rows="3"
                                       placeholder="Add a note..."></textarea>
                         </div>
                         <!-- Read-only provenance — who made it, when -->
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Author:</label>
                             <span id="prop-markup-author" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                         </div>
                         <div class="prop-row">
                             <label>Created:</label>
                             <span id="prop-markup-timestamp" class="prop-value-muted">—</span>
                         </div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Appearance</div>
                     <div class="prop-field">
                         <label for="prop-stroke-color">Color</label>
                         <input type="color" id="prop-stroke-color" value="#ff0000">
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-field">
                         <label for="prop-stroke-width">Width</label>
                         <input type="range" id="prop-stroke-width"
                                min="1" max="10" value="2" step="1">
                         <span id="prop-stroke-width-val" class="range-val">2</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>

                 <!-- Document info — shown when no markup is selected -->
                 <div id="doc-info" style="display: none;">
                     <div class="prop-section-header">Document</div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>File:</label>
                         <span id="prop-filename">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Pages:</label>
                         <span id="prop-pages">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Size:</label>
                         <span id="prop-size">—</span>
                     </div>
                     <div class="prop-row">
                         <label>Page Dims:</label>
                         <span id="prop-dims">—</span>
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <p id="no-doc-msg" class="muted-text">Open a PDF to see properties</p>
             </div>
             </div>
             <!-- Plugin tab content divs injected here by PluginLoader._injectTab() -->
         </div>
     </div>

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- BOTTOM STATUS BAR                                                       -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="status-bar">
         <span id="status-page">Page: — / —</span>
         <span id="status-zoom">Zoom: 100%</span>
         <!-- Drawing scale selector — set to match the title block scale on the drawing -->
         <span id="status-scale" class="status-scale" style="display: none;" title="Drawing scale — set to match
     the title block">
             <label for="scale-select" class="scale-prefix">Scale:</label>
             <!-- scale-label: shown as text when a preset is active (read by _updateStatusBar) -->
             <span id="scale-label" class="scale-label-text" style="display:none;"></span>
             <select id="scale-select" class="scale-select-input">
                 <option value="unscaled">Unscaled</option>
                 <!-- Hidden option for programmatic selection when calibration sets custom scale -->
                 <option value="custom" style="display:none;">Custom</option>
                 <optgroup label="Architectural">
                     <option value="arch_3_32">3/32" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_8">1/8" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_16">3/16" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_4">1/4" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_8">3/8" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_2">1/2" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_4">3/4" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_1">1" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_1_half">1-1/2" = 1'</option>
                     <option value="arch_3_1">3" = 1'</option>
                 </optgroup>
                 <optgroup label="Engineering">
                     <option value="eng_1_10">1" = 10'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_20">1" = 20'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_30">1" = 30'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_40">1" = 40'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_50">1" = 50'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_60">1" = 60'</option>
                     <option value="eng_1_100">1" = 100'</option>
                 </optgroup>
             </select>
         </span>
         <span id="status-counts" class="status-counts" style="display: none;">
             <span class="status-count-item count-issue" title="Issues">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-question" title="Questions">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-approval" title="Approvals">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-change" title="Changes">0</span>
             <span class="status-count-sep">|</span>
             <span class="status-count-item count-open" title="Open markups">0 open</span>
         </span>
         <span id="status-message"></span>
     </div>

     <!-- Hidden file input for PDF upload -->
     <input type="file" id="file-input" accept=".pdf" style="display: none;">

     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- NEW DOCUMENT MODAL                                                      -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-new-doc" class="modal" style="display: none;" role="dialog" aria-modal="true"
     aria-labelledby="modal-new-doc-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-new-doc-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box">
             <h2 id="modal-new-doc-title">New Document</h2>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-name">Name</label>
                 <input type="text" id="new-doc-name" value="Untitled" maxlength="120"
                        placeholder="Document name" autocomplete="off">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-pages">Pages</label>
                 <input type="number" id="new-doc-pages" value="1" min="1" max="50">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="new-doc-size">Page Size</label>
                 <select id="new-doc-size">
                     <option value="letter">Letter (8.5" × 11")</option>
                     <option value="tabloid">Tabloid (11" × 17")</option>
                     <option value="arch_c">Arch C (18" × 24")</option>
                     <option value="arch_d">Arch D (24" × 36")</option>
                     <option value="arch_e">Arch E (36" × 48")</option>
                     <option value="a4">A4 (8.3" × 11.7")</option>
                     <option value="legal">Legal (8.5" × 14")</option>
                 </select>
             </div>
             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="new-doc-cancel" class="toolbar-btn">Cancel</button>
                 <button id="new-doc-create" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Create</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- CALIBRATION MODAL                                                       -->
     <!-- Shown after the user draws a reference line with the Calibrate tool.   -->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-calibrate" class="modal" style="display: none;"
          role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="modal-calibrate-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-calibrate-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box">
             <h2 id="modal-calibrate-title">Calibrate Scale</h2>
             <p class="modal-hint">Draw a line over a known dimension, then enter its real length below.</p>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="calib-pixels">Measured Length (pixels)</label>
                 <input type="text" id="calib-pixels" readonly
                        placeholder="—" style="opacity: 0.7; cursor: default;">
             </div>
             <div class="modal-field">
                 <label for="calib-value">Real-World Length</label>
                 <div class="calib-input-row">
                     <input type="number" id="calib-value" min="0.01" step="any"
                            placeholder="e.g. 20" style="flex: 1;">
                     <select id="calib-unit" style="flex: 0 0 auto; width: 100px;">
                         <option value="ft">feet</option>
                         <option value="in">inches</option>
                         <option value="m">meters</option>
                         <option value="cm">centimeters</option>
                     </select>
                 </div>
             </div>
             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="calib-cancel" class="toolbar-btn">Cancel</button>
                 <button id="calib-apply" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Apply Scale</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <!-- TOOLBAR SETTINGS MODAL                                              -->
     <!-- Allows per-tool show/hide with checkboxes, persisted in localStorage-->
     <!-- ====================================================================== -->
     <div id="modal-toolbar-settings" class="modal" style="display: none;"
          role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="modal-settings-title">
         <div class="modal-overlay" id="modal-settings-overlay"></div>
         <div class="modal-box modal-settings-box">
             <h2 id="modal-settings-title">Toolbar Settings</h2>
             <p class="modal-hint">Choose which tools appear in each tab.</p>

             <div class="settings-tab-sections">
                 <!-- Navigate tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Navigate</div>
                     <div id="settings-navigate-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically by toolbar.js -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Markup tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Markup</div>
                     <div id="settings-markup-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
                 <!-- Measure tools -->
                 <div class="settings-section">
                     <div class="settings-section-header">Measure</div>
                     <div id="settings-measure-tools" class="settings-tool-list">
                         <!-- Populated dynamically -->
                     </div>
                 </div>
             </div>

             <div class="modal-buttons">
                 <button id="settings-reset" class="toolbar-btn">Reset to Default</button>
                 <button id="settings-close" class="toolbar-btn modal-btn-primary">Done</button>
             </div>
         </div>
     </div>


     <!-- Fabric.js 6.9.1 — UMD bundle, sets globalThis.fabric -->
     <script src="/static/lib/fabric.min.js"></script>
     <!-- Main application (ES module) — loads after Fabric.js is available -->
     <script src="/static/js/app.js" type="module"></script>

     </body>
     </html>

● Now Phase 1: Bundle Export/Import. Let me read the relevant files first:

  ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  The .portolan ZIP format uses Python's io.BytesIO() as an in-memory file buffer — no temp file needed. zipfile.ZipFile
   writes directly into the BytesIO, then buf.getvalue() extracts the bytes for the HTTP response. This is faster and
  safer than writing to disk. The ZIP_DEFLATED compression typically achieves 60-80% size reduction for text JSON
  payloads (markups, layers, scale) while leaving the PDF mostly unchanged (PDFs are already compressed).
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
