  speed while keeping the model choice configurable. The plan feels solid, so I'm ready to present it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Ready to code?

 Here is Claude's plan:
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 PAI Agent Infrastructure — Claude Proxy + Ollama MCP Bridge

 Date: 2026-02-24
 Goal: Route Fabric/PAI text generation through Claude Code's existing auth; expose
 Ollama local models as MCP tools so Claude can explicitly delegate cheap/bulk work to them.

 ---
 Context

 PAI currently uses Ollama (mistral:7b) for all text generation. This means every Fabric
 pattern, every AI summary, every analysis goes through a 7B local model. The user wants
 Claude-quality output for everything that matters — without a separate API key — and wants
 the ability to consciously delegate mechanical/bulk work to smaller local models to save cost
 and latency.

 The solution is two components:
 1. A Claude Proxy — an OpenAI-compatible local server that shells out to claude -p,
 letting any OpenAI-API-speaking tool (Fabric, PortolanCAST) use Claude with no API key.
 2. An Ollama MCP Server — exposes Ollama models as callable MCP tools so I (Claude Code)
 can explicitly delegate cheap tasks inline during any session.

 The delegation doctrine (to be added to THEDELEGATIONSYSTEM.md):

 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┐
 │    Delegate to Ollama (small model)     │       Keep with Claude        │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ Summarize large docs before reading     │ Architectural decisions       │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ Bulk text classification                │ Code writing / implementation │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ Pattern extraction from repetitive data │ Strategy, counsel, synthesis  │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ Pre-filtering long logs/lists           │ Novel problem solving         │
 ├─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ MemoryVault embeddings (stays, always)  │ Final judgment calls          │
 └─────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘

 ---
 Architecture

 Your Terminal
   ├── fabric → ClaudeProxy (127.0.0.1:11435) → claude -p [Claude models]
   ├── PortolanCAST EC plugin → ClaudeProxy (11435) → claude -p [Claude models]
   ├── MemoryVault → Ollama (127.0.0.1:11434) → nomic-embed-text [stays, no change]
   └── Claude Code session
         └── MCP: ollama-bridge
               ├── ollama_list_models()
               ├── ollama_generate(model, prompt, system_prompt)
               ├── ollama_summarize(text, model)
               ├── ollama_classify(text, categories, model)
               └── memory_search(query, n, collection)

 ---
 Component 1: Claude Proxy Server

 Location: ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/
 Files: server.py, run.sh
 Runtime: ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 (has uvicorn, pydantic, httpx already)
 Port: 11435 (no conflict with Ollama 11434 or PortolanCAST 8000)

 server.py design

 FastAPI app with two endpoints:

 POST /v1/chat/completions — OpenAI-compatible chat
 1. Parse request: model, messages[], stream (v1 ignores streaming, always sync)
 2. Extract system message (role=system) → --system-prompt
 3. Flatten remaining messages into a single prompt string:
 User: {content}
 Assistant: {content}
 User: {content}
 4. Map model name → claude flag:
   - opus* / claude-opus* → --model opus
   - haiku* / claude-haiku* → --model haiku
   - anything else → --model sonnet (safe default)
 5. Call:
 subprocess.run(
     ['claude', '-p', '--no-session-persistence',
      '--output-format', 'text',
      '--model', model_flag,
      '--system-prompt', system_text,
      user_prompt],
     capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120
 )
 6. Return OpenAI-format JSON response:
 {
   "id": "chatcmpl-...",
   "object": "chat.completion",
   "choices": [{
     "index": 0,
     "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": "<response text>"},
     "finish_reason": "stop"
   }],
   "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
 }

 GET /v1/models — List available models (required by some clients)
 Returns a static list: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5

 GET /health — Health check, returns {"status": "ok"}

 Security:
 - Bind to 127.0.0.1 only (localhost, no network exposure)
 - No auth needed (local-only service)
 - Subprocess timeout: 120s (Claude is sometimes slow on complex prompts)

 run.sh design

 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 # Claude Proxy — run OUTSIDE of any Claude Code session
 # unset CLAUDECODE prevents the nested-session guard from blocking claude -p calls
 unset CLAUDECODE
 exec ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/server.py

 The unset CLAUDECODE is the documented bypass for the nested session guard. If the user
 starts the proxy from a terminal that has CLAUDECODE set (e.g. a terminal opened from
 within Claude Code's Bash tool), this ensures the proxy's claude -p calls still work.

 ---
 Component 2: Ollama MCP Server

 Location: ~/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/
 Files: server.py
 Runtime: ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 (after pip install mcp)
 Transport: stdio (MCP standard — Claude Code launches it automatically)

 Prerequisite: Install mcp package

 ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/pip install mcp

 server.py — 5 tools

 Tool 1: ollama_list_models
 - No input
 - Calls GET http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags
 - Returns: list of {name, parameter_size, size_gb} for each model
 - Description: "List all Ollama models available locally. Use this before delegating to
 confirm which model to use."

 Tool 2: ollama_generate
 - Input: model (str), prompt (str), system_prompt (str, optional)
 - Calls POST /api/generate with stream: false
 - Returns: response text
 - Description: "Run a prompt through a local Ollama model. Use for mechanical tasks that
 don't require Claude-level reasoning: repetitive extraction, simple Q&A on structured
 data, format conversion. Available models: mistral:7b (4.3GB), qwen2.5:14b (8.9GB),
 gemma2:27b (15.6GB)."

 Tool 3: ollama_summarize
 - Input: text (str, long input), model (str, default mistral:7b),
 max_words (int, default 200)
 - Builds summarization system prompt internally
 - Calls ollama_generate internally
 - Returns: condensed summary
 - Description: "Summarize a long document or text using a local model. Use this BEFORE
 reading a large file yourself — get a digest first, then decide if you need the full text."

 Tool 4: ollama_classify
 - Input: text (str), categories (list[str]), model (str, default mistral:7b)
 - Builds classification prompt: "Classify the following text into exactly one of these
 categories: {categories}. Reply with only the category name."
 - Returns: category string
 - Description: "Classify text into one of the provided categories using a local model.
 Good for tagging markups, routing items, sorting logs."

 Tool 5: memory_search
 - Input: query (str), n (int, default 5), collection (str, default all)
 - Calls MemoryVault.py search via subprocess:
 subprocess.run([VENV_PYTHON, MEMORYVAULT_PY, 'search', query,
                 '--n', str(n), '--collection', collection], ...)
 - Returns: list of results with text + metadata
 - Description: "Search the PAI semantic memory (past sessions, decisions, council outputs).
 Use this to recall previous conversations, past architectural decisions, or context from
 earlier work sessions. Always search memory when the user references something from the past."

 Why memory_search in MCP is powerful

 Currently, to search memory, I have to shell out via Bash. With an MCP tool, I can call it
 inline — it appears in my tool list naturally, and I can search automatically when context
 from past sessions might be relevant, without requiring a separate Bash step.

 ---
 Component 3: Settings.json Update

 Add mcpServers block to ~/.claude/settings.json:

 {
   "env": { ...existing... },
   "hooks": { ...existing... },
   "mcpServers": {
     "ollama-bridge": {
       "command": "/home/odikral/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3",
       "args": ["/home/odikral/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/server.py"],
       "env": {
         "OLLAMA_URL": "http://127.0.0.1:11434",
         "PAI_DIR": "/home/odikral/.claude"
       }
     }
   }
 }

 The MCP server starts automatically when Claude Code opens a session. No manual startup needed.

 ---
 Component 4: Fabric Config Update

 Edit ~/.config/fabric/.env:

 # CHANGED: route through Claude Proxy instead of Ollama
 DEFAULT_VENDOR=OpenAI
 DEFAULT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6
 OPENAI_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1
 OPENAI_API_KEY=claude-proxy

 # UNCHANGED: keep Ollama for embed model
 OLLAMA_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434
 OLLAMA_API_KEY=
 CUSTOM_PATTERNS_DIRECTORY=/home/odikral/.config/custom-fabric-patterns

 Note: Fabric's OpenAI vendor reads OPENAI_API_BASE_URL to override the endpoint. The
 OPENAI_API_KEY must be non-empty (any string works — the proxy ignores it).

 ---
 Component 5: PortolanCAST EC Plugin Update (Bonus)

 /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/PortolanCAST/main.py — ai_summary endpoint:

 Change the Ollama call to use the proxy's OpenAI-compatible endpoint:
 # OLD: r = req_lib.post("http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",
 #          json={"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False})
 # NEW:
 r = req_lib.post(
     "http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1/chat/completions",
     json={
         "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
         "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
         "stream": False,
     },
     timeout=60,
 )
 summary = r.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"].strip()
 mode = "ai"

 No change to the fallback logic — if the proxy is offline, computed summary still works.

 ---
 Component 6: Delegation Doctrine Update

 Append to ~/.claude/skills/CORE/SYSTEM/THEDELEGATIONSYSTEM.md:

 A new section "Local Model Delegation (Ollama MCP Bridge)" covering:
 - When to call ollama_summarize vs reading a file directly
 - When to call ollama_classify vs reasoning yourself
 - Always calling memory_search for past-session context
 - Never delegating reasoning/judgment/synthesis to Ollama
 - The "pyramid": Ollama for speed/bulk → Claude for judgment/final output

 ---
 File Summary

 ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┐
 │                          File                          │            Action             │
 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/server.py                  │ NEW — FastAPI proxy server    │
 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh                     │ NEW — startup script          │
 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ ~/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/server.py          │ NEW — MCP stdio server        │
 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
 │ ~/.claude/settings.json                                │ ADD mcpServers block          │
 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤

 ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤

 ---
 Verification

 1. Proxy smoke test:
 bash ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh &
 curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11435/health
 curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1/chat/completions \
   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   -d '{"model":"sonnet","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say exactly: proxy works"}]}'
 2. Fabric smoke test:
 echo "test input" | ~/go/bin/fabric -p summarize
 # Should use Claude via proxy, not mistral:7b
 3. MCP tools test (within Claude Code session):
   - New session: MCP tools should appear in tool list as mcp__ollama-bridge__*
   - Call ollama_list_models → should return mistral:7b, qwen2.5:14b, gemma2:27b
   - Call memory_search("PortolanCAST", 3) → should return recent session entries
 4. PortolanCAST AI summary test:
   - Start PortolanCAST server, open a doc with markups
   - Click Refresh in EC Brief panel
   - Should return Claude-quality narrative (not mistral)

 ---
 Gotchas

 - unset CLAUDECODE in run.sh is mandatory — without it, claude -p fails with nested session error
 - Fabric's OPENAI_API_BASE_URL must end in /v1 — Fabric appends /chat/completions itself
 - OPENAI_API_KEY must be non-empty in Fabric — use any placeholder string
 - The mcp Python package must be installed into MEMORY venv, not system Python (PEP 668)
 - MCP server stdout is the protocol channel — never print to stdout in server.py (use stderr for logs)
 - memory_search MCP tool calls MemoryVault.py via subprocess — Ollama must be running for embed
 - PortolanCAST EC plugin: proxy must be running before the AI summary endpoint is called
 - Model name mapping in proxy: be permissive — unknown model names default to sonnet safely
 - Proxy subprocess.run timeout should be 120s (long Opus calls can take 30-60s on complex prompts)

│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ GET /v1/models — List available models (required by some clients)                                                    │
│ Returns a static list: claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ GET /health — Health check, returns {"status": "ok"}                                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Security:                                                                                                            │
│ - Bind to 127.0.0.1 only (localhost, no network exposure)                                                            │
│ - No auth needed (local-only service)                                                                                │
│ - Subprocess timeout: 120s (Claude is sometimes slow on complex prompts)                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ run.sh design                                                                                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ #!/usr/bin/env bash                                                                                                  │
│ # Claude Proxy — run OUTSIDE of any Claude Code session                                                              │
│ # unset CLAUDECODE prevents the nested-session guard from blocking claude -p calls                                   │
│ unset CLAUDECODE                                                                                                     │
│ exec ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/server.py                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ The unset CLAUDECODE is the documented bypass for the nested session guard. If the user                              │
│ starts the proxy from a terminal that has CLAUDECODE set (e.g. a terminal opened from                                │
│ within Claude Code's Bash tool), this ensures the proxy's claude -p calls still work.                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Component 2: Ollama MCP Server                                                                                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Location: ~/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/                                                                       │
│ Files: server.py                                                                                                     │
│ Runtime: ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 (after pip install mcp)                                                   │
│ Transport: stdio (MCP standard — Claude Code launches it automatically)                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Prerequisite: Install mcp package                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/pip install mcp                                                                            │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ server.py — 5 tools                                                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Tool 1: ollama_list_models                                                                                           │
│ - No input                                                                                                           │
│ - Calls GET http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags                                                                          │
│ - Returns: list of {name, parameter_size, size_gb} for each model                                                    │
│ - Description: "List all Ollama models available locally. Use this before delegating to                              │
│ confirm which model to use."                                                                                         │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Tool 2: ollama_generate                                                                                              │
│ - Input: model (str), prompt (str), system_prompt (str, optional)                                                    │
│ - Calls POST /api/generate with stream: false                                                                        │
│ - Returns: response text                                                                                             │
│ - Description: "Run a prompt through a local Ollama model. Use for mechanical tasks that                             │
│ don't require Claude-level reasoning: repetitive extraction, simple Q&A on structured                                │
│ data, format conversion. Available models: mistral:7b (4.3GB), qwen2.5:14b (8.9GB),                                  │
│ gemma2:27b (15.6GB)."                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Tool 3: ollama_summarize                                                                                             │
│ - Input: text (str, long input), model (str, default mistral:7b),                                                    │
│ max_words (int, default 200)                                                                                         │
│ - Builds summarization system prompt internally                                                                      │
│ - Calls ollama_generate internally                                                                                   │
│ - Returns: condensed summary                                                                                         │
│ - Description: "Summarize a long document or text using a local model. Use this BEFORE                               │
│ reading a large file yourself — get a digest first, then decide if you need the full text."                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Tool 4: ollama_classify                                                                                              │
│ - Input: text (str), categories (list[str]), model (str, default mistral:7b)                                         │
│ - Builds classification prompt: "Classify the following text into exactly one of these                               │
│ categories: {categories}. Reply with only the category name."                                                        │
│ - Returns: category string                                                                                           │
│ - Description: "Classify text into one of the provided categories using a local model.                               │
│ Good for tagging markups, routing items, sorting logs."                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Tool 5: memory_search                                                                                                │
│ - Input: query (str), n (int, default 5), collection (str, default all)                                              │
│ - Calls MemoryVault.py search via subprocess:                                                                        │
│ subprocess.run([VENV_PYTHON, MEMORYVAULT_PY, 'search', query,                                                        │
│                 '--n', str(n), '--collection', collection], ...)                                                     │
│ - Returns: list of results with text + metadata                                                                      │
│ - Description: "Search the PAI semantic memory (past sessions, decisions, council outputs).                          │
│ Use this to recall previous conversations, past architectural decisions, or context from                             │
│ earlier work sessions. Always search memory when the user references something from the past."                       │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Why memory_search in MCP is powerful                                                                                 │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Currently, to search memory, I have to shell out via Bash. With an MCP tool, I can call it                           │
│ inline — it appears in my tool list naturally, and I can search automatically when context                           │
│ from past sessions might be relevant, without requiring a separate Bash step.                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Component 3: Settings.json Update                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Add mcpServers block to ~/.claude/settings.json:                                                                     │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ {                                                                                                                    │
│   "env": { ...existing... },                                                                                         │
│   "hooks": { ...existing... },                                                                                       │
│   "mcpServers": {                                                                                                    │
│     "ollama-bridge": {                                                                                               │
│       "command": "/home/odikral/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3",                                                    │
│       "args": ["/home/odikral/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/server.py"],                                         │
│       "env": {                                                                                                       │
│         "OLLAMA_URL": "http://127.0.0.1:11434",                                                                      │
│         "PAI_DIR": "/home/odikral/.claude"                                                                           │
│       }                                                                                                              │
│     }                                                                                                                │
│   }                                                                                                                  │
│ }                                                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ The MCP server starts automatically when Claude Code opens a session. No manual startup needed.                      │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Component 4: Fabric Config Update                                                                                    │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Edit ~/.config/fabric/.env:                                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ # CHANGED: route through Claude Proxy instead of Ollama                                                              │
│ DEFAULT_VENDOR=OpenAI                                                                                                │
│ DEFAULT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6                                                                                      │
│ OPENAI_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1                                                                        │
│ OPENAI_API_KEY=claude-proxy                                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ # UNCHANGED: keep Ollama for embed model                                                                             │
│ OLLAMA_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434                                                                                │
│ OLLAMA_API_KEY=                                                                                                      │
│ CUSTOM_PATTERNS_DIRECTORY=/home/odikral/.config/custom-fabric-patterns                                               │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Note: Fabric's OpenAI vendor reads OPENAI_API_BASE_URL to override the endpoint. The                                 │
│ OPENAI_API_KEY must be non-empty (any string works — the proxy ignores it).                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Component 5: PortolanCAST EC Plugin Update (Bonus)                                                                   │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ /mnt/c/Users/User1/ClaudeProjects/PortolanCAST/main.py — ai_summary endpoint:                                        │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ Change the Ollama call to use the proxy's OpenAI-compatible endpoint:                                                │
│ # OLD: r = req_lib.post("http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate",                                                       │
│ #          json={"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt, "stream": False})                                            │
│ # NEW:                                                                                                               │
│ r = req_lib.post(                                                                                                    │
│     "http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1/chat/completions",                                                                    │
│     json={                                                                                                           │
│         "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",                                                                                │
│         "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],                                                           │
│         "stream": False,                                                                                             │
│     },                                                                                                               │
│     timeout=60,                                                                                                      │
│ )                                                                                                                    │
│ summary = r.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"].strip()                                                       │
│ mode = "ai"                                                                                                          │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ No change to the fallback logic — if the proxy is offline, computed summary still works.                             │
│                                                                                                                      │

│   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \                                                                              │
│   -d '{"model":"sonnet","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say exactly: proxy works"}]}'                          │

│ # Should use Claude via proxy, not mistral:7b                                                                        │
│ 3. MCP tools test (within Claude Code session):                                                                      │
│   - New session: MCP tools should appear in tool list as mcp__ollama-bridge__*                                       │
│   - Call ollama_list_models → should return mistral:7b, qwen2.5:14b, gemma2:27b                                      │
│   - Call memory_search("PortolanCAST", 3) → should return recent session entries                                     │
│ 4. PortolanCAST AI summary test:                                                                                     │
│   - Start PortolanCAST server, open a doc with markups                                                               │
│   - Click Refresh in EC Brief panel                                                                                  │
│   - Should return Claude-quality narrative (not mistral)                                                             │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ ---                                                                                                                  │
│ Gotchas                                                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                      │
│ - unset CLAUDECODE in run.sh is mandatory — without it, claude -p fails with nested session error                    │
│ - Fabric's OPENAI_API_BASE_URL must end in /v1 — Fabric appends /chat/completions itself                             │
│ - OPENAI_API_KEY must be non-empty in Fabric — use any placeholder string                                            │
│ - The mcp Python package must be installed into MEMORY venv, not system Python (PEP 668)                             │
│ - MCP server stdout is the protocol channel — never print to stdout in server.py (use stderr for logs)               │
│ - memory_search MCP tool calls MemoryVault.py via subprocess — Ollama must be running for embed                      │
│ - PortolanCAST EC plugin: proxy must be running before the AI summary endpoint is called                             │
│ - Model name mapping in proxy: be permissive — unknown model names default to sonnet safely                          │
│ - Proxy subprocess.run timeout should be 120s (long Opus calls can take 30-60s on complex prompts)                   │

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  OpenAI-compatibility shim pattern: The proxy wraps claude -p subprocess calls in the OpenAI Chat Completions schema —
  no new auth tokens, no extra infrastructure. Any tool that speaks OpenAI API (Fabric, LangChain, etc.) transparently
  gets Claude output. The unset CLAUDECODE trick is documented Claude Code behavior: the env var is the session guard,
  not a process lock.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ⎿  Wrote 339 lines to /home/odikral/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/server.py
        1 """
        2 ClaudeProxy — OpenAI-Compatible Proxy for Claude Code CLI
        3
        4 Purpose:
        5     Presents an OpenAI Chat Completions-compatible HTTP API on localhost:11435.
        6     Translates each request into a `claude -p` subprocess call, so any tool that
        7     speaks the OpenAI API (Fabric, PortolanCAST, LangChain, etc.) can use Claude
        8     without a separate API key or network account.
        9
       10 Security assumptions:
       11     - Binds to 127.0.0.1 only — zero network exposure
       12     - No authentication needed (localhost-only service)
       13     - subprocess timeout of 120s protects against infinite hang
       14     - Caller auth token (OPENAI_API_KEY) is accepted but ignored
       15
       16 Threat model:
       17     - Local-only service; not exposed to the internet
       18     - The subprocess call uses a fixed argument list — no shell=True,
       19       no dynamic shell interpolation, so injection is not possible
       20     - Long prompts could consume large amounts of memory; mitigated by
       21       subprocess timeout and no streaming buffer accumulation
       22
       23 Author: PAI Infrastructure
       24 Version: 1.0.0
       25 Date: 2026-02-24
       26
       27 Usage:
       28     # From a terminal outside Claude Code:
       29     bash ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh
       30
       31     # Smoke test:
       32     curl http://127.0.0.1:11435/health
       33     curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1/chat/completions \
       34       -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
       35       -d '{"model":"sonnet","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say: proxy works"}]}'
       36 """
       37
       38 import subprocess
       39 import sys
       40 import time
       41 import uuid
       42 import os
       43
       44 from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
       45 from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
       46 import uvicorn
       47
       48 # =============================================================================
       49 # APPLICATION SETUP
       50 # =============================================================================
       51
       52 app = FastAPI(title="ClaudeProxy", version="1.0.0")
       53
       54 # Port — defined once so run.sh and this file stay in sync
       55 PROXY_PORT = 11435
       56
       57 # Maximum seconds to wait for a claude -p call.
       58 # Opus on a complex prompt can take 30-60s; 120s gives comfortable headroom.
       59 SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT = 120
       60
       61 # Static model list returned by /v1/models — used by Fabric and similar clients
       62 # that check available models before sending a request.
       63 AVAILABLE_MODELS = [
       64     {"id": "claude-sonnet-4-6", "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       65     {"id": "claude-opus-4-6",   "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       66     {"id": "claude-haiku-4-5",  "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       67     # Friendly aliases — Fabric and other clients often request these
       68     {"id": "sonnet",            "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       69     {"id": "opus",              "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       70     {"id": "haiku",             "object": "model", "created": 1700000000, "owned_by": "anthropic"},
       71 ]
       72
       73
       74 # =============================================================================
       75 # MODEL NAME MAPPING
       76 # =============================================================================
       77
       78 def _map_model(requested: str | None) -> str:
       79     """
       80     Map a requested model name to the claude CLI --model flag value.
       81
       82     Claude Code's -p flag accepts: 'opus', 'sonnet', 'haiku' as shorthand.
       83     We normalise any reasonable alias so Fabric and other tools that send
       84     arbitrary model names still get routed correctly.
       85
       86     Args:
       87         requested: The model field from the incoming OpenAI-format request.
       88                    May be None, an alias, or a full model ID.
       89
       90     Returns:
       91         A string suitable for the `--model` flag: 'opus', 'sonnet', or 'haiku'.
       92     """
       93     if not requested:
       94         return "sonnet"  # safe default for unspecified models
       95
       96     r = requested.lower()
       97
       98     # Opus variants
       99     if "opus" in r:
      100         return "opus"
      101
      102     # Haiku variants
      103     if "haiku" in r:
      104         return "haiku"
      105
      106     # Sonnet variants (also the fallback for unknown names)
      107     # Reason: sonnet is the best balance of quality and speed for most tasks.
      108     # An unknown model name is safer to default to sonnet than to haiku.
      109     return "sonnet"
      110
      111
      112 # =============================================================================
      113 # MESSAGE FLATTENING
      114 # =============================================================================
      115
      116 def _extract_system_and_prompt(messages: list[dict]) -> tuple[str, str]:
      117     """
      118     Split an OpenAI messages array into a system prompt and a flat user prompt.
      119
      120     The claude -p interface takes:
      121       --system-prompt <text>   (optional)
      122       <positional prompt>      (required)
      123
      124     We reconstruct the conversation history as a plain-text transcript for the
      125     positional prompt, which is what most single-turn Fabric calls need anyway.
      126     Multi-turn history is preserved for tools that send it (e.g. PortolanCAST).
      127
      128     Args:
      129         messages: List of {"role": str, "content": str} dicts, OpenAI format.
      130
      131     Returns:
      132         (system_text, user_prompt) — either may be an empty string.
      133     """
      134     system_parts: list[str] = []
      135     conversation_parts: list[str] = []
      136
      137     for msg in messages:
      138         role = msg.get("role", "user")
      139         content = msg.get("content", "")
      140         if not isinstance(content, str):
      141             # Some clients send content as a list of blocks (vision, tool use).
      142             # Flatten to plain text — we're a text-only proxy.
      143             content = " ".join(
      144                 part.get("text", "") if isinstance(part, dict) else str(part)
      145                 for part in content
      146             )
      147
      148         if role == "system":
      149             system_parts.append(content)
      150         elif role == "assistant":
      151             # Include prior assistant turns for context in multi-turn conversations
      152             conversation_parts.append(f"Assistant: {content}")
      153         else:
      154             # user, tool, function — treat as user
      155             conversation_parts.append(f"User: {content}")
      156
      157     system_text = "\n\n".join(system_parts)
      158     # For single-user-message calls (the common case), omit the "User: " prefix
      159     # so the prompt looks natural. For multi-turn, include all roles.
      160     if len(conversation_parts) == 1 and conversation_parts[0].startswith("User: "):
      161         user_prompt = conversation_parts[0][len("User: "):]
      162     else:
      163         user_prompt = "\n".join(conversation_parts)
      164
      165     return system_text, user_prompt
      166
      167
      168 # =============================================================================
      169 # ENDPOINTS
      170 # =============================================================================
      171
      172 @app.get("/health")
      173 def health():
      174     """
      175     Health check endpoint.
      176
      177     Used by run.sh startup wait loop and external smoke tests to verify
      178     the server is accepting connections.
      179     """
      180     return {"status": "ok", "service": "ClaudeProxy", "port": PROXY_PORT}
      181
      182
      183 @app.get("/v1/models")
      184 def list_models():
      185     """
      186     OpenAI-compatible model listing endpoint.
      187
      188     Some clients (Fabric with certain vendor configs, LangChain) call this
      189     before sending completions to validate the model name. We return a static
      190     list of the Claude models this proxy supports.
      191     """
      192     return {
      193         "object": "list",
      194         "data": AVAILABLE_MODELS,
      195     }
      196
      197
      198 @app.post("/v1/chat/completions")
      199 async def chat_completions(request: Request):
      200     """
      201     OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint.
      202
      203     Receives an OpenAI-format chat request, translates it into a claude -p
      204     subprocess call, and wraps the response in OpenAI format.
      205
      206     The claude CLI is invoked with:
      207         claude -p --no-session-persistence --output-format text
      208                --model <mapped> [--system-prompt <text>] <prompt>
      209
      210     Args:
      211         request: FastAPI Request containing JSON body with:
      212                  - model (str, optional): model identifier
      213                  - messages (list[dict]): OpenAI messages array
      214                  - stream (bool, optional): ignored — always returns sync response
      215
      216     Returns:
      217         JSONResponse in OpenAI chat.completion format, or error JSON with
      218         appropriate HTTP status on failure.
      219
      220     Security:
      221         - subprocess uses a fixed argument list (no shell=True)
      222         - system prompt and user prompt are passed as discrete arguments,
      223           not interpolated into a shell string — injection is not possible
      224         - stderr from claude is captured and logged to proxy stderr only,
      225           never forwarded to the caller
      226     """
      227     try:
      228         body = await request.json()
      229     except Exception as e:
      230         return JSONResponse({"error": f"Invalid JSON: {e}"}, status_code=400)
      231
      232     messages = body.get("messages", [])
      233     model_requested = body.get("model")
      234
      235     if not messages:
      236         return JSONResponse({"error": "messages array is required"}, status_code=400)
      237
      238     # Map caller's model name to a claude CLI flag
      239     model_flag = _map_model(model_requested)
      240
      241     # Split system prompt from user conversation
      242     system_text, user_prompt = _extract_system_and_prompt(messages)
      243
      244     if not user_prompt.strip():
      245         return JSONResponse({"error": "No user message content found"}, status_code=400)
      246
      247     # Build subprocess argument list — deliberately NOT using shell=True
      248     cmd = [
      249         "claude", "-p",
      250         "--no-session-persistence",
      251         "--output-format", "text",
      252         "--model", model_flag,
      253     ]
      254
      255     # Only add --system-prompt if we have one; the flag requires a value
      256     if system_text.strip():
      257         cmd += ["--system-prompt", system_text]
      258
      259     # User prompt is the positional argument — last in the list
      260     cmd.append(user_prompt)
      261
      262     print(f"[ClaudeProxy] Calling: model={model_flag} prompt_len={len(user_prompt)}", file=sys.stderr)
      263
      264     try:
      265         result = subprocess.run(
      266             cmd,
      267             capture_output=True,
      268             text=True,
      269             timeout=SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT,
      270         )
      271     except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
      272         print(f"[ClaudeProxy] Timeout after {SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT}s", file=sys.stderr)
      273         return JSONResponse(
      274             {"error": f"Claude subprocess timed out after {SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT}s"},
      275             status_code=504,
      276         )
      277     except FileNotFoundError:
      278         # claude binary not on PATH — happens if proxy started outside Claude Code env
      279         print("[ClaudeProxy] ERROR: 'claude' binary not found on PATH", file=sys.stderr)
      280         return JSONResponse(
      281             {"error": "'claude' binary not found. Is Claude Code installed and on PATH?"},
      282             status_code=500,
      283         )
      284     except Exception as e:
      285         print(f"[ClaudeProxy] Subprocess error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
      286         return JSONResponse({"error": str(e)}, status_code=500)
      287
      288     if result.returncode != 0:
      289         stderr_snippet = result.stderr[:500] if result.stderr else "(no stderr)"
      290         print(f"[ClaudeProxy] claude returned non-zero: {result.returncode}\n{stderr_snippet}", file=sy
          s.stderr)
      291         # Still try to return whatever stdout we got; sometimes claude writes
      292         # the response and then exits non-zero due to session guard warnings.
      293         if not result.stdout.strip():
      294             return JSONResponse(
      295                 {"error": f"claude exited {result.returncode}: {stderr_snippet}"},
      296                 status_code=500,
      297             )
      298
      299     response_text = result.stdout.strip()
      300
      301     # Build OpenAI-format response
      302     completion_id = f"chatcmpl-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
      303     model_id_map = {"opus": "claude-opus-4-6", "haiku": "claude-haiku-4-5", "sonnet": "claude-sonnet-4-
          6"}
      304     model_id = model_id_map.get(model_flag, "claude-sonnet-4-6")
      305
      306     return JSONResponse({
      307         "id": completion_id,
      308         "object": "chat.completion",
      309         "created": int(time.time()),
      310         "model": model_id,
      311         "choices": [
      312             {
      313                 "index": 0,
      314                 "message": {
      315                     "role": "assistant",
      316                     "content": response_text,
      317                 },
      318                 "finish_reason": "stop",
      319             }
      320         ],
      321         "usage": {
      322             # Approximate token counts — claude -p doesn't expose exact counts.
      323             # Clients that require exact usage should use the Anthropic API directly.
      324             "prompt_tokens": len(user_prompt) // 4,
      325             "completion_tokens": len(response_text) // 4,
      326             "total_tokens": (len(user_prompt) + len(response_text)) // 4,
      327         },
      328     })
      329
      330
      331 # =============================================================================
      332 # ENTRY POINT
      333 # =============================================================================
      334
      335 if __name__ == "__main__":
      336     print(f"[ClaudeProxy] Starting on http://127.0.0.1:{PROXY_PORT}", file=sys.stderr)
      337     print("[ClaudeProxy] IMPORTANT: Start this from a terminal where 'claude' is on PATH", file=sys.std
          err)
      338     print("[ClaudeProxy] IMPORTANT: CLAUDECODE env var must NOT be set (run.sh handles this)", file=sys
          .stderr)
      339     uvicorn.run(app, host="127.0.0.1", port=PROXY_PORT, log_level="warning")

● Write(/home/odikral/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh)
  ⎿  Wrote 59 lines to /home/odikral/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh
       1 #!/usr/bin/env bash
       2 # =============================================================================
       3 # ClaudeProxy — Startup Script
       4 # =============================================================================
       5 #
       6 # Purpose:
       7 #   Starts the ClaudeProxy server on http://127.0.0.1:11435
       8 #   in a clean environment that allows nested claude -p calls.
       9 #
      10 # CRITICAL: Run this from a terminal OUTSIDE Claude Code.
      11 #   If you run it from within a Claude Code session's Bash tool, the
      12 #   subprocess calls inside the proxy will fail with a nested-session error.
      13 #   This script unsets CLAUDECODE to prevent that, but it's still safer
      14 #   to start it from a dedicated terminal.
      15 #
      16 # Usage:
      17 #   bash ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh
      18 #
      19 # Background usage:
      20 #   bash ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh &
      21 #   curl http://127.0.0.1:11435/health  # wait for "ok"
      22 #
      23 # Stop:
      24 #   kill $(lsof -ti :11435)
      25 #
      26 # =============================================================================
      27
      28 set -euo pipefail
      29
      30 # SECURITY: unset CLAUDECODE — this is the documented bypass for the nested
      31 # session guard. Without this, claude -p calls from inside the proxy would
      32 # be blocked by Claude Code's protection against recursive sessions.
      33 unset CLAUDECODE
      34
      35 # Python from the MEMORY venv — has uvicorn, fastapi, pydantic already installed.
      36 # Using the venv python avoids PEP 668 restrictions on system python.
      37 VENV_PYTHON="$HOME/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3"
      38 PROXY_SCRIPT="$HOME/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/server.py"
      39
      40 # Validate dependencies before starting
      41 if [[ ! -x "$VENV_PYTHON" ]]; then
      42     echo "[ClaudeProxy] ERROR: venv python not found at $VENV_PYTHON" >&2
      43     echo "[ClaudeProxy] Run: ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/pip install uvicorn fastapi" >&2
      44     exit 1
      45 fi
      46
      47 if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
      48     echo "[ClaudeProxy] ERROR: 'claude' command not found on PATH" >&2
      49     echo "[ClaudeProxy] Make sure Claude Code CLI is installed and on your PATH" >&2
      50     exit 1
      51 fi
      52
      53 echo "[ClaudeProxy] Starting on http://127.0.0.1:11435"
      54 echo "[ClaudeProxy] CLAUDECODE unset: $(echo ${CLAUDECODE:-unset})"
      55 echo "[ClaudeProxy] Python: $VENV_PYTHON"
      56 echo "[ClaudeProxy] Press Ctrl+C to stop"
      57 echo ""
      58
      59 exec "$VENV_PYTHON" "$PROXY_SCRIPT"

● Bash(chmod +x ~/.claude/tools/ClaudeProxy/run.sh && echo "chmod ok")
  ⎿  chmod ok

        1 """
        2 Ollama MCP Bridge — Exposes Ollama Local Models as MCP Tools
        3
        4 Purpose:
        5     An MCP (Model Context Protocol) stdio server that gives Claude Code
        6     direct access to local Ollama models and the PAI MemoryVault.
        7     Claude can explicitly delegate cheap/bulk work to small local models
        8     without leaving the session or shelling out manually.
        9
       10 Security assumptions:
       11     - Only talks to localhost:11434 (Ollama) and localhost filesystem (MemoryVault)
       12     - No network exposure — stdio transport only
       13     - Subprocess calls use fixed argument lists (no shell=True)
       14     - All external data returned to Claude is plain text strings
       15
       16 Threat model:
       17     - Ollama responses are from a local model — not trusted as authoritative
       18     - MemoryVault subprocess output could be large; truncated at 8KB per result
       19     - No secrets in this file; Ollama URL comes from OLLAMA_URL env var
       20
       21 Author: PAI Infrastructure
       22 Version: 1.0.0
       23 Date: 2026-02-24
       24
       25 Usage:
       26     # Registered in ~/.claude/settings.json mcpServers block.
       27     # Claude Code starts this automatically when a session opens.
       28     # DO NOT start manually — MCP uses stdio, not HTTP.
       29
       30     # Test locally:
       31     echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}' | \
       32       OLLAMA_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434 PAI_DIR=/home/odikral/.claude \
       33       /path/to/venv/python3 server.py
       34 """
       35
       36 import json
       37 import os
       38 import subprocess
       39 import sys
       40 from typing import Any
       41
       42 # =============================================================================
       43 # MCP IMPORT — mcp package must be installed in this venv
       44 # =============================================================================
       45
       46 try:
       47     from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP
       48 except ImportError:
       49     print(
       50         "ERROR: 'mcp' package not found. Run:\n"
       51         "  ~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/pip install mcp",
       52         file=sys.stderr,
       53     )
       54     sys.exit(1)
       55
       56 # =============================================================================
       57 # CONFIGURATION — from environment variables set in settings.json mcpServers
       58 # =============================================================================
       59
       60 # Ollama base URL — MCP server receives this from the env block in settings.json
       61 OLLAMA_URL = os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "http://127.0.0.1:11434")
       62
       63 # PAI directory — used to locate MemoryVault.py
       64 PAI_DIR = os.environ.get("PAI_DIR", os.path.expanduser("~/.claude"))
       65
       66 # Path to the MemoryVault Python script
       67 MEMORYVAULT_PY = os.path.join(PAI_DIR, "skills", "CORE", "Tools", "MemoryVault.py")
       68
       69 # Path to the venv python (for MemoryVault subprocess calls)
       70 VENV_PYTHON = os.path.join(PAI_DIR, "MEMORY", "venv", "bin", "python3")
       71
       72 # Default Ollama model for summarize/classify tasks.
       73 # mistral:7b is fast (4.3GB) and good enough for mechanical tasks.
       74 DEFAULT_MODEL = "mistral:7b"
       75
       76 # Maximum characters of MemoryVault output to return — prevents context flooding
       77 MEMORY_RESULT_MAX_CHARS = 8000
       78
       79 # MCP stdio server instance
       80 mcp = FastMCP("ollama-bridge")
       81
       82
       83 # =============================================================================
       84 # INTERNAL HELPERS
       85 # =============================================================================
       86
       87 def _ollama_post(endpoint: str, payload: dict, timeout: int = 60) -> dict:
       88     """
       89     POST to the Ollama HTTP API and return the parsed JSON response.
       90
       91     All Ollama calls go through this function so connection errors are
       92     handled consistently. Never raises — returns an error dict instead,
       93     so MCP tools can return structured error messages to Claude.
       94
       95     Args:
       96         endpoint: API path, e.g. '/api/generate' or '/api/tags'
       97         payload:  JSON-serializable dict for the request body
       98         timeout:  Request timeout in seconds (default 60; generation can be slow)
       99
      100     Returns:
      101         Parsed JSON dict, or {"error": <message>} on failure.
      102     """
      103     import urllib.request
      104     import urllib.error
      105
      106     url = OLLAMA_URL.rstrip("/") + endpoint
      107     data = json.dumps(payload).encode("utf-8")
      108
      109     try:
      110         req = urllib.request.Request(
      111             url,
      112             data=data,
      113             headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
      114             method="POST",
      115         )
      116         with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
      117             return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
      118     except urllib.error.URLError as e:
      119         # Ollama is offline or wrong port
      120         return {"error": f"Ollama unreachable at {url}: {e}"}
      121     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
      122         return {"error": f"Invalid JSON from Ollama: {e}"}
      123     except Exception as e:
      124         return {"error": f"Ollama request failed: {e}"}
      125
      126
      127 def _ollama_get(endpoint: str, timeout: int = 10) -> dict:
      128     """
      129     GET from the Ollama HTTP API and return the parsed JSON response.
      130
      131     Args:
      132         endpoint: API path, e.g. '/api/tags'
      133         timeout:  Request timeout in seconds
      134
      135     Returns:
      136         Parsed JSON dict, or {"error": <message>} on failure.
      137     """
      138     import urllib.request
      139     import urllib.error
      140
      141     url = OLLAMA_URL.rstrip("/") + endpoint
      142     try:
      143         with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=timeout) as resp:
      144             return json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
      145     except urllib.error.URLError as e:
      146         return {"error": f"Ollama unreachable at {url}: {e}"}
      147     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
      148         return {"error": f"Invalid JSON from Ollama: {e}"}
      149     except Exception as e:
      150         return {"error": f"Ollama GET failed: {e}"}
      151
      152
      153 # =============================================================================
      154 # TOOL 1: ollama_list_models
      155 # =============================================================================
      156
      157 @mcp.tool()
      158 def ollama_list_models() -> str:
      159     """
      160     List all Ollama models available locally.
      161
      162     USE THIS before delegating to confirm which model to use.
      163     Returns name, parameter count, and disk size for each installed model.
      164     Typically available: mistral:7b (4.3GB, fast), qwen2.5:14b (8.9GB, capable),
      165     gemma2:27b (15.6GB, high quality).
      166
      167     Returns:
      168         JSON string listing available models with their sizes, or an error message.
      169     """
      170     result = _ollama_get("/api/tags")
      171
      172     if "error" in result:
      173         return f"Error: {result['error']}\nIs Ollama running? Try: ollama serve"
      174
      175     models = result.get("models", [])
      176     if not models:
      177         return "No models installed. Run: ollama pull mistral:7b"
      178
      179     output = []
      180     for m in models:
      181         name = m.get("name", "unknown")
      182         # Parameter size is in the details dict, size is bytes
      183         details = m.get("details", {})
      184         param_size = details.get("parameter_size", "?")
      185         size_bytes = m.get("size", 0)
      186         size_gb = size_bytes / (1024 ** 3) if size_bytes else 0
      187         output.append(f"  {name:<30} {param_size:<8}  {size_gb:.1f} GB")
      188
      189     return "Available Ollama models:\n" + "\n".join(output)
      190
      191
      192 # =============================================================================
      193 # TOOL 2: ollama_generate
      194 # =============================================================================
      195
      196 @mcp.tool()
      197 def ollama_generate(
      198     prompt: str,
      199     model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
      200     system_prompt: str = "",
      201 ) -> str:
      202     """
      203     Run a prompt through a local Ollama model.
      204
      205     USE FOR mechanical tasks that don't require Claude-level reasoning:
      206     - Repetitive text extraction or reformatting
      207     - Simple Q&A on structured data
      208     - Format conversion (JSON → Markdown, etc.)
      209     - Pre-filtering or preliminary triage of long content
      210
      211     DO NOT USE FOR: architectural decisions, code writing, strategy, synthesis,
      212     final judgment calls, or anything where quality matters to the user.
      213
      214     Available models (typical install): mistral:7b, qwen2.5:14b, gemma2:27b.
      215     Check with ollama_list_models() first if unsure.
      216
      217     Args:
      218         prompt:        The prompt to send to the model.
      219         model:         Ollama model name (default: mistral:7b).
      220         system_prompt: Optional system prompt for role/context setting.
      221
      222     Returns:
      223         The model's response text, or an error message.
      224     """
      225     payload: dict[str, Any] = {
      226         "model": model,
      227         "prompt": prompt,
      228         "stream": False,  # IMPORTANT: stream=False gives us one complete response
      229     }
      230     if system_prompt.strip():
      231         payload["system"] = system_prompt
      232
      233     result = _ollama_post("/api/generate", payload, timeout=120)
      234
      235     if "error" in result:
      236         return f"Error from Ollama: {result['error']}"
      237
      238     response = (result.get("response") or "").strip()
      239     if not response:
      240         return "Ollama returned an empty response."
      241
      242     return response
      243
      244
      245 # =============================================================================
      246 # TOOL 3: ollama_summarize
      247 # =============================================================================
      248
      249 @mcp.tool()
      250 def ollama_summarize(
      251     text: str,
      252     model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
      253     max_words: int = 200,
      254 ) -> str:
      255     """
      256     Summarize a long document or text using a local Ollama model.
      257
      258     USE THIS before reading a large file directly. Get a digest first,
      259     then decide if you need the full content. Saves context window space
      260     and speeds up responses dramatically for large inputs.
      261
      262     Good for: session logs, long markdown files, large JSON outputs,
      263     error logs, changelogs, any text > 2KB you might want to summarize first.
      264
      265     Args:
      266         text:      The text to summarize. Can be very long.
      267         model:     Ollama model to use (default: mistral:7b).
      268         max_words: Target word count for the summary (default: 200).
      269
      270     Returns:
      271         A concise summary of the input text.
      272     """
      273     system_prompt = (
      274         f"You are a precise summarizer. "
      275         f"Summarize the provided text in {max_words} words or fewer. "
      276         "Focus on the most important information. "
      277         "Do not include preamble, commentary, or meta-statements about the summary. "
      278         "Just the summary."
      279     )
      280     prompt = f"Summarize the following text:\n\n{text}"
      281
      282     return ollama_generate(prompt=prompt, model=model, system_prompt=system_prompt)
      283
      284
      285 # =============================================================================
      286 # TOOL 4: ollama_classify
      287 # =============================================================================
      288
      289 @mcp.tool()
      290 def ollama_classify(
      291     text: str,
      292     categories: list[str],
      293     model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
      294 ) -> str:
      295     """
      296     Classify text into one of the provided categories using a local model.
      297
      298     USE FOR: tagging markups by type, routing items to queues, sorting logs
      299     into severity buckets, categorizing user feedback, labeling document sections.
      300
      301     The local model is sufficient for this task — classification is mechanical
      302     and doesn't require Claude-level reasoning.
      303
      304     Args:
      305         text:       The text to classify.
      306         categories: List of valid category names (2-20 items recommended).
      307         model:      Ollama model to use (default: mistral:7b).
      308
      309     Returns:
      310         The chosen category name as a single string, or an error message.
      311     """
      312     if not categories:
      313         return "Error: categories list must not be empty."
      314
      315     category_list = ", ".join(f'"{c}"' for c in categories)
      316     system_prompt = (
      317         "You are a text classifier. "
      318         f"Classify the input into EXACTLY ONE of these categories: {category_list}. "
      319         "Reply with ONLY the category name. No explanation, no punctuation, no quotes."
      320     )
      321     prompt = f"Classify this text:\n\n{text}"
      322
      323     response = ollama_generate(prompt=prompt, model=model, system_prompt=system_prompt)
      324
      325     # Try to match the response to one of the provided categories (case-insensitive)
      326     # in case the model adds extra whitespace or minor formatting.
      327     response_clean = response.strip().strip('"\'').lower()
      328     for cat in categories:
      329         if cat.lower() == response_clean:
      330             return cat  # Return in original case
      331
      332     # If no exact match, return the raw response — the model may have
      333     # provided a close paraphrase; let Claude interpret it.
      334     return response
      335
      336
      337 # =============================================================================
      338 # TOOL 5: memory_search
      339 # =============================================================================
      340
      341 @mcp.tool()
      342 def memory_search(
      343     query: str,
      344     n: int = 5,
      345     collection: str = "all",
      346 ) -> str:
      347     """
      348     Search the PAI semantic memory for past sessions, decisions, and council outputs.
      349
      350     ALWAYS USE THIS when the user references something from a past conversation,
      351     asks about a previous decision, mentions earlier work on a project, or when
      352     context from prior sessions might be relevant to the current task.
      353
      354     Collections available:
      355       - "sessions"   — past Claude Code session summaries
      356       - "decisions"  — architectural and strategic decisions
      357       - "council"    — Counsel skill outputs (archetypes, war council)
      358       - "all"        — search all collections (default)
      359
      360     Requires: Ollama must be running (for embedding the query with nomic-embed-text).
      361
      362     Args:
      363         query:      Semantic search query (natural language, not keywords).
      364         n:          Number of results to return (default: 5, max: 20).
      365         collection: Which MemoryVault collection to search (default: "all").
      366
      367     Returns:
      368         Formatted search results with text snippets and metadata, or an error.
      369     """
      370     if not os.path.exists(MEMORYVAULT_PY):
      371         return (
      372             f"MemoryVault not found at {MEMORYVAULT_PY}. "
      373             "Is the MEMORY venv set up? Check ~/.claude/MEMORY/."
      374         )
      375
      376     # Clamp n to a reasonable range to avoid flooding the context window
      377     n = max(1, min(n, 20))
      378
      379     cmd = [
      380         VENV_PYTHON,
      381         MEMORYVAULT_PY,
      382         "search",
      383         query,
      384         "--n", str(n),
      385     ]
      386     # Only add --collection if not "all" (MemoryVault may not support the flag for all)
      387     if collection != "all":
      388         cmd += ["--collection", collection]
      389
      390     try:
      391         result = subprocess.run(
      392             cmd,
      393             capture_output=True,
      394             text=True,
      395             timeout=30,  # Embedding + search should be well within 30s
      396         )
      397     except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
      398         return "memory_search timed out (>30s). Is Ollama running?"
      399     except Exception as e:
      400         return f"memory_search subprocess error: {e}"
      401
      402     output = result.stdout.strip()
      403     if result.returncode != 0:
      404         stderr_snippet = result.stderr[:500] if result.stderr else "(no stderr)"
      405         return f"MemoryVault error (exit {result.returncode}): {stderr_snippet}"
      406
      407     if not output:
      408         return f"No results found for query: {query!r}"
      409
      410     # Truncate to prevent flooding Claude's context window
      411     if len(output) > MEMORY_RESULT_MAX_CHARS:
      412         output = output[:MEMORY_RESULT_MAX_CHARS] + f"\n... [truncated, {len(output)} chars total]"
      413
      414     return output
      415
      416
      417 # =============================================================================
      418 # ENTRY POINT — stdio transport (MCP standard)
      419 # =============================================================================
      420
      421 if __name__ == "__main__":
      422     # CRITICAL: Never print to stdout here — stdout IS the MCP protocol channel.
      423     # All logging must go to stderr.
      424     print(
      425         f"[ollama-bridge] Starting MCP server. Ollama URL: {OLLAMA_URL}",
      426         file=sys.stderr,
      427     )
      428     # mcp.run() uses stdio transport by default — correct for settings.json integration
      429     mcp.run()

∴ Thinking…

  mcp 1.26.0 is installed. Now let me check if FastMCP is in that version - earlier versions may have a different API.

● Bash(~/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 -c "from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP; print('FastMCP OK')" 2>&1)
  ⎿  FastMCP OK

● ★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
  MCP stdio transport: The MCP protocol uses stdin/stdout as its channel — the server process communicates JSON-RPC over
   the pipe that Claude Code manages. This is why the server MUST NOT print to stdout (even debug logs), and why it
  can't be started manually like a normal server. The settings.json mcpServers block makes Claude Code own the process
  lifecycle automatically.
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Now update settings.json, fabric/.env, and THEDELEGATIONSYSTEM.md — all independent:

∴ Thinking…

  The settings.json schema doesn't have mcpServers as a field in the user settings.json. It mentions
  enabledMcpjsonServers and disabledMcpjsonServers, but mcpServers is not in the schema validation.

  Looking at the schema more carefully, the additionalProperties: {} at the top level means it will accept additional
  properties. Wait, the error says "Unrecognized field: mcpServers" but the root schema says "additionalProperties": {}.
   Let me look at this more carefully...

  Actually, the error says "Unrecognized field: mcpServers" and the schema has "additionalProperties": {} which means
  additional properties ARE allowed.

  But the Edit tool failed because Claude Code's own validator is rejecting it. Looking at the Claude Code
  documentation, MCP servers are actually configured in .claude/mcp.json or ~/.claude/mcp.json at the project or global
  level, not in settings.json. The plan I had was adding it to the wrong file entirely.

  Checking the schema more carefully, I see references to enabledMcpjsonServers and disabledMcpjsonServers which point
  to .mcp.json files as the actual location for MCP server definitions. So I need to configure the servers in those
  separate config files instead. I need to verify the exact location of the MCP configuration file before creating it,
  since I'm not entirely certain whether it should be ~/.claude/mcp.json or if there's already one in place.

∴ Thinking…

  I can see the structure of ~/.claude.json. There's a projects key with per-project settings, and there's a mcpServers
  key inside the project settings. Looking at the structure more carefully:

  "projects": {
    "/mnt/c/Users/User1": {
      "allowedTools": [],
      "mcpContextUris": [],
      "mcpServers": {},
      ...
    }
  }

  The mcpServers is nested inside the project. For user-level (global) MCP servers, according to the guide tool, they
  would be at the top level in ~/.claude.json as "mcpServers": {...}.

  I need to add a top-level mcpServers block to the config file, being careful not to touch the sensitive oauthAccount
  data that's already there. Since there's no global mcpServers key yet, I'll need to create one at the root level of
  the JSON.

  Actually, directly editing .claude.json is risky given the sensitive account information it contains and the potential
   for formatting errors. The safer approach is to use the claude mcp add CLI command with the --scope user flag, which
  handles the file modifications automatically and ensures everything gets written to the correct location.

  Now I'm checking the exact syntax for the command, particularly how to pass the Python script path as an argument and
  include the environment variables for the Ollama URL and PAI directory. I'm realizing the CLI approach is much safer
  than manually editing JSON with sensitive credentials. Let me look up what the claude mcp command offers.

● The MCP config lives in ~/.claude.json. The safest way is claude mcp add CLI to avoid touching sensitive account data:

● Bash(claude mcp add --help 2>&1 | head -40)
  ⎿  Usage: claude mcp add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]

     Add an MCP server to Claude Code.

     Examples:
       # Add HTTP server:
       claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp

       # Add HTTP server with headers:
       claude mcp add --transport http corridor https://app.corridor.dev/api/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer
     ..."

       # Add stdio server with environment variables:
       claude mcp add -e API_KEY=xxx my-server -- npx my-mcp-server

       # Add stdio server with subprocess flags:
       claude mcp add my-server -- my-command --some-flag arg1

     Options:
       --callback-port <port>       Fixed port for OAuth callback (for servers
                                    requiring pre-registered redirect URIs)
       --client-id <clientId>       OAuth client ID for HTTP/SSE servers
       --client-secret              Prompt for OAuth client secret (or set
                                    MCP_CLIENT_SECRET env var)
       -e, --env <env...>           Set environment variables (e.g. -e KEY=value)
       -H, --header <header...>     Set WebSocket headers (e.g. -H "X-Api-Key:
                                    abc123" -H "X-Custom: value")
       -h, --help                   Display help for command
       -s, --scope <scope>          Configuration scope (local, user, or project)
                                    (default: "local")
       -t, --transport <transport>  Transport type (stdio, sse, http). Defaults to
                                    stdio if not specified.

● Bash(claude mcp add --scope user \
        -e OLLAMA_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434 \
        -e PAI_DIR=/home/odikral/.claude \
        ollama-bridge -- \
        /home/odikral/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 \
        /home/odikral/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/server.py 2>&1)

     KEY2=value2

● Bash(claude mcp add --scope user ollama-bridge \

        -- /home/odikral/.claude/MEMORY/venv/bin/python3 \
        /home/odikral/.claude/mcp-servers/ollama-bridge/server.py 2>&1)
